<content>
1: import { readFile, writeFile } from "node:fs/promises";
2: 
3: import { BenchmarkResult, EvolutionCandidate, SimulationWeights, TeacherPolicy } from "./types.js";
4: import { Prng } from "./random.js";
5: import { DEFAULT_POLICY, DEFAULT_WEIGHTS, clampPolicy, clampWeights } from "./policy.js";
6: import { benchmarkToMarkdown, runBenchmarkSuite } from "./benchmark.js";
7: import { mutateScalar, mutatePolicy, mutateWeights } from "./mutation.js";
8: 
9: interface EvolutionArchive {
10:   currentPolicy: TeacherPolicy;
11:   bestScore: number;
12:   candidates: Array<{
13:     policy: TeacherPolicy;
14:     score: number;
15:     novelty: number;
16:     accepted: boolean;
17:     iteration: number;
18:   }>;
19: }
20: 
21: export interface EvolutionRun {
22:   baseline: BenchmarkResult;
23:   best: BenchmarkResult;
24:   acceptedCandidates: EvolutionCandidate[];
25:   exploredCandidates: EvolutionCandidate[];
26:   archive: EvolutionArchive;
27:   metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
28: }
29: 
30: function diffPolicy(before: TeacherPolicy, after: TeacherPolicy) {
31:   const keys: Array<keyof TeacherPolicy> = [
32:     "analogyDensity",
33:     "socraticRatio",
34:     "formalism",
35:     "retrievalPractice",
36:     "exerciseCount",
37:     "diagramBias",
38:     "reflectionBias",
39:     "interdisciplinaryBias",
40:     "challengeRate"
41:   ];
42:   return keys
43:     .map((field) => {
44:       const previous = before[field];
45:       const next = after[field];
46:       const delta = typeof previous === "number" && typeof next === "number" ? next - previous : 0;
47:       return { field, before: previous, after: next, delta };
48:     })
49:     .filter((entry) => entry.delta !== 0);
50: }
51: 
52: function policyVector(policy: TeacherPolicy): number[] {
53:   return [
54:     policy.analogyDensity,
55:     policy.socraticRatio,
56:     policy.formalism,
57:     policy.retrievalPractice,
58:     policy.exerciseCount / 5,
59:     policy.diagramBias,
60:     policy.reflectionBias,
61:     policy.interdisciplinaryBias,
62:     policy.challengeRate
63:   ];
64: }
65: 
66: function euclideanDistance(a: number[], b: number[]): number {
67:   let sum = 0;
68:   for (let i = 0; i < a.length; i++) {
69:     sum += (a[i] - b[i]) ** 2;
70:   }
71:   return Math.sqrt(sum / a.length);
72: }
73: 
74: export function noveltyScore(existingPolicies: TeacherPolicy[], candidate: TeacherPolicy): number {
75:   if (existingPolicies.length === 0) return 1;
76:   const candidateVec = policyVector(candidate);
77:   let minDist = Infinity;
78:   for (const existing of existingPolicies) {
79:     const dist = euclideanDistance(candidateVec, policyVector(existing));
80:     minDist = Math.min(minDist, dist);
81:   }
82:   return minDist;
83: }
84: 
85: async function loadArchive(filePath: string): Promise<EvolutionArchive> {
86:   try {
87:     const content = await readFile(filePath, "utf8");
88:     return JSON.parse(content) as EvolutionArchive;
89:   } catch {
90:     return {
91:       currentPolicy: DEFAULT_POLICY,
92:       bestScore: 0,
93:       candidates: []
94:     };
95:   }
96: }
97: 
98: async function saveArchive(filePath: string, archive: EvolutionArchive): Promise<void> {
99:   await writeFile(filePath, `${JSON.stringify(archive, null, 2)}\n`, "utf8");
100: }
101: 
102: export async function evolvePolicy(
103:   archivePath: string,
104:   basePolicy: TeacherPolicy,
105:   focusTopic?: string,
106:   iterations = 24,
107:   seed = 20260401,
108:   baseWeights: SimulationWeights = DEFAULT_WEIGHTS
109: ): Promise<EvolutionRun> {
110:   const archive = await loadArchive(archivePath);
111:   const baseline = await runBenchmarkSuite(process.cwd(), basePolicy, focusTopic, seed, 3, baseWeights);
112:   let best = baseline;
113:   let bestWeights = baseWeights;
114:   const acceptedCandidates: EvolutionCandidate[] = [];
115:   const exploredCandidates: EvolutionCandidate[] = [];
116:   const prng = new Prng(seed + 17);
117:   const seen: TeacherPolicy[] = [...archive.candidates.map((entry) => entry.policy), basePolicy];
118: 
119:   for (let iteration = 1; iteration <= iterations; iteration += 1) {
120:     const candidatePolicy = mutatePolicy(best.policy, prng, iteration, "keating-candidate");
121:     const candidateWeights = mutateWeights(bestWeights, prng);
122:     const novelty = noveltyScore(seen, candidatePolicy);
123:     const candidateBenchmark = await runBenchmarkSuite(process.cwd(), candidatePolicy, focusTopic, seed + iteration * 11, 3, candidateWeights);
124:     const parameterDelta = diffPolicy(best.policy, candidatePolicy);
125:     const candidate: EvolutionCandidate = {
126:       policy: candidatePolicy,
127:       benchmark: candidateBenchmark,
128:       parentName: best.policy.name,
129:       iteration,
130:       novelty,
131:       accepted: false,
132:       parameterDelta,
133:       decision: {
134:         improves: false,
135:         safe: false,
136:         novelEnough: false,
137:         scoreDelta: 0,
138:         weakestTopicDelta: 0,
139:         reasons: []
140:       }
141:     };
142: 
143:     const bestWeakest = Math.min(...best.topicBenchmarks.map((entry) => entry.meanScore));
144:     const candidateWeakest = Math.min(...candidateBenchmark.topicBenchmarks.map((entry) => entry.meanScore));
145:     const improves = candidateBenchmark.overallScore > best.overallScore;
146:     const safe = candidateWeakest >= bestWeakest - 1.5;
147:     const novelEnough = novelty >= 0.05;
148:     candidate.decision.improves = improves;
149:     candidate.decision.safe = safe;
150:     candidate.decision.novelEnough = novelEnough;
151:     candidate.decision.scoreDelta = candidateBenchmark.overallScore - best.overallScore;
152:     candidate.decision.weakestTopicDelta = candidateWeakest - bestWeakest;
153:     if (improves) {
154:       candidate.decision.reasons.push(
155:         `overall score improved by ${candidate.decision.scoreDelta.toFixed(2)}`
156:       );
157:     } else {
158:       candidate.decision.reasons.push(
159:         `overall score regressed by ${Math.abs(candidate.decision.scoreDelta).toFixed(2)}`
160:       );
161:     }
162:     if (safe) {
163:       candidate.decision.reasons.push(
164:         `weakest-topic score stayed within tolerance (${candidate.decision.weakestTopicDelta.toFixed(2)})`
165:       );
166:     } else {
167:       candidate.decision.reasons.push(
168:         `weakest-topic score fell too far (${candidate.decision.weakestTopicDelta.toFixed(2)})`
169:       );
170:     }
171:     if (novelEnough) {
172:       candidate.decision.reasons.push(`novelty ${novelty.toFixed(3)} cleared the 0.05 threshold`);
173:     } else {
174:       candidate.decision.reasons.push(`novelty ${novelty.toFixed(3)} was too close to archived policies`);
175:     }
176: 
177:     if (improves && safe && novelEnough) {
178:       candidate.accepted = true;
179:       best = candidateBenchmark;
180:       bestWeights = candidateWeights;
181:       acceptedCandidates.push(candidate);
182:     }
183:     exploredCandidates.push(candidate);
184:     seen.push(candidate.policy);
185:   }
186: 
187:   const nextArchive: EvolutionArchive = {
188:     currentPolicy: best.policy,
189:     bestScore: best.overallScore,
190:     candidates: [
191:       ...archive.candidates,
192:       ...exploredCandidates.map((entry) => ({
193:         policy: entry.policy,
194:         score: entry.benchmark.overallScore,
195:         novelty: entry.novelty,
196:         accepted: entry.accepted,
197:         iteration: entry.iteration
198:       }))
199:     ]
200:   };
201: 
202:   await saveArchive(archivePath, nextArchive);
203:   return {
204:     baseline,
205:     best,
206:     acceptedCandidates,
207:     exploredCandidates,
208:     archive: nextArchive
209:   };
210: }
211: 
212: export function evolutionToMarkdown(run: EvolutionRun): string {
213:   const lines = [
214:     `# Evolution Report: ${run.best.policy.name}`,
215:     "",
216:     `- Baseline score: ${run.baseline.overallScore.toFixed(2)}`,
217:     `- Best score: ${run.best.overallScore.toFixed(2)}`,
218:     `- Accepted candidates: ${run.acceptedCandidates.length}`,
219:     `- Explored candidates: ${run.exploredCandidates.length}`,
220:     ...(run.metadata?.optimizerUsed ? [`- Optimizer used: ${String(run.metadata.optimizerUsed)}`] : []),
221:     ""
222:   ];
223: 
224:   lines.push("## Accepted Candidates");
225:   lines.push("");
226:   if (run.acceptedCandidates.length === 0) {
227:     lines.push("- No candidate cleared both the novelty and safety gates in this run.");
228:   } else {
229:     for (const candidate of run.acceptedCandidates) {
230:       lines.push(
231:         `- Iteration ${candidate.iteration}: ${candidate.policy.name} scored ${candidate.benchmark.overallScore.toFixed(2)} with novelty ${candidate.novelty.toFixed(3)}.`
232:       );
233:     }
234:   }
235: 
236:   lines.push("");
237:   lines.push("## Decision Ledger");
238:   lines.push("");
239:   for (const candidate of run.exploredCandidates) {
240:     lines.push(
241:       `- Iteration ${candidate.iteration} ${candidate.policy.name}: ${candidate.accepted ? "accepted" : "rejected"}`
242:     );
243:     lines.push(`  - score delta: ${candidate.decision.scoreDelta.toFixed(2)}`);
244:     lines.push(`  - weakest-topic delta: ${candidate.decision.weakestTopicDelta.toFixed(2)}`);
245:     lines.push(`  - novelty: ${candidate.novelty.toFixed(3)}`);
246:     lines.push(`  - gates: improves=${candidate.decision.improves}, safe=${candidate.decision.safe}, novelEnough=${candidate.decision.novelEnough}`);
247:     lines.push(`  - reasons: ${candidate.decision.reasons.join("; ")}`);
248:     if (candidate.parameterDelta.length > 0) {
249:       lines.push(
250:         `  - parameter delta: ${candidate.parameterDelta
251:           .map((entry) => `${entry.field}:${entry.delta >= 0 ? "+" : ""}${entry.delta.toFixed(2)}`)
252:           .join(", ")}`
253:       );
254:     }
255:   }
256: 
257:   lines.push("");
258:   lines.push("## Best Benchmark Snapshot");
259:   lines.push("");
260:   lines.push(benchmarkToMarkdown(run.best).trim());
261:   lines.push("");
262:   return `${lines.join("\n")}\n`;
263: }
264: 
265: import { optimizePolicy, OptimizePolicyOptions } from "./ax-optimizer.js";
266: import { mapElitesEvolve, MapElitesOptions, mapElitesToEvolutionRun, mapElitesToMarkdown } from "./map-elites.js";
267: 
268: export async function evolveWithGEPA(
269:   cwd: string,
270:   basePolicy: TeacherPolicy,
271:   options?: OptimizePolicyOptions
272: ): Promise<EvolutionRun> {
273:   return optimizePolicy(cwd, basePolicy, options);
274: }
275: 
276: export { mapElitesEvolve, mapElitesToEvolutionRun, mapElitesToMarkdown };
277: export type { MapElitesOptions } from "./map-elites.js";

(End of file - total 277 lines)

64: const SESSION_RESTORE_TIMEOUT_MS = 5_000;
65: async function browserPersistentStorageGranted(): Promise<boolean> {
66:   if (typeof navigator === "undefined" || !navigator.storage?.persisted) return false;
67:   try {
68:     return await navigator.storage.persisted();
69:   } catch {
70:     return false;
71:   }
72: }
73: 
74: async function requestBrowserPersistentStorage(): Promise<PersistentStorageStatus> {
75:   if (typeof navigator === "undefined" || !navigator.storage) return "declined";
76:   try {
77:     if (navigator.storage.persisted && await navigator.storage.persisted()) {
78:       return "granted";
79:     }
80:     if (!navigator.storage.persist) return "declined";
81:     const granted = await navigator.storage.persist();
82:     if (granted) return "granted";
83:     return await browserPersistentStorageGranted() ? "granted" : "declined";
84:   } catch {
85:     return "declined";
86:   }
87: }
88: 
89: async function withSessionRestoreTimeout<T>(operation: Promise<T>, label: string): Promise<T> {
90:   let timeoutId: number | undefined;
91:   const timeout = new Promise<never>((_, reject) => {
92:     timeoutId = window.setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(`${label} timed out after ${SESSION_RESTORE_TIMEOUT_MS}ms`)), SESSION_RESTORE_TIMEOUT_MS);
93:   });
94: 
95:   try {
96:     return await Promise.race([operation, timeout]);
97:   } finally {
98:     if (timeoutId !== undefined) window.clearTimeout(timeoutId);
99:   }
100: }

37: const ANSI_RE = /\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]/g;
38: 
39: function visibleWidth(text: string): number {
40:  return text.replace(ANSI_RE, "").length;
41: }
42: 
43: function truncateVisible(text: string, width: number): string {
44:  const limit = Math.max(0, width);
45:  if (visibleWidth(text) <= limit) return text;
46:  if (limit === 0) return "";
47: 
48:  const suffix = limit > 1 ? "…" : "";
49:  const contentLimit = Math.max(0, limit - visibleWidth(suffix));
50:  let visible = 0;
51:  let output = "";
52: 
53:  for (let index = 0; index < text.length;) {
54:  const ansi = text.slice(index).match(/^\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]/);
55:  if (ansi) {
56:  output += ansi[0];
57:  index += ansi[0].length;
58:  continue;
59:  }
60: 
61:  if (visible >= contentLimit) break;
62:  output += text[index];
63:  visible += 1;
64:  index += 1;
65:  }
66: 
67:  return `${output}${suffix}`;
68: }
69: 
70: function padVisible(text: string, width: number): string {
71:  const fitted = truncateVisible(text, width);
72:  const vw = visibleWidth(fitted);
73:  return vw >= width ? fitted : fitted + " ".repeat(width - vw);
74: }
75: 
76: function truncatePlain(text: string, width: number): string {
77:  if (text.length <= width) return text;
78:  if (width <= 1) return text.slice(0, Math.max(0, width));
79:  return `${text.slice(0, width - 1)}…`;
80: }
81: 
82: function centerPlain(text: string, width: number): string {
83:  const truncated = truncatePlain(text, width);
84:  const gap = Math.max(0, width - truncated.length);
85:  const left = Math.floor(gap / 2);
86:  return `${" ".repeat(left)}${truncated}${" ".repeat(gap - left)}`;
87: }
88: 
89: function wrapWords(text: string, maxWidth: number): string[] {
90:  const width = Math.max(1, maxWidth);
91:  const words = text.split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean);
92:  const lines: string[] = [];
93:  let current = "";
94: 
95:  for (const raw of words) {
96:  const word = truncatePlain(raw, width);
97:  const next = current ? `${current} ${word}` : word;
98:  if (current && next.length > width) {
99:  lines.push(current);
100:  current = word;
101:  } else {
102:  current = next;
103:  }
104:  }
105: 
106:  if (current) lines.push(current);
107:  return lines.length > 0 ? lines : [""];
108: }
109: 
110: function formatHeaderPath(path: string): string {
111:  const home = homedir();
112:  return path.startsWith(home) ? `~${path.slice(home.length)}` : path;
113: }
114: 
115: function getCurrentModelLabel(ctx: any): string {
116:  if (typeof ctx?.model === "string" && ctx.model.trim()) return ctx.model.trim();
117:  if (ctx?.model?.provider && ctx?.model?.id) return `${ctx.model.provider}/${ctx.model.id}`;
118: 
119:  const branch = ctx?.sessionManager?.getBranch?.();
120:  if (Array.isArray(branch)) {
121:  for (let index = branch.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
122:  const entry = branch[index];
123:  if (entry?.type === "model_change" && entry.provider && entry.modelId) {
124:  return `${entry.provider}/${entry.modelId}`;
125:  }
126:  }
127:  }
128: 
129:  return "not set";
130: }
131: 
132: function getSessionLabel(ctx: any): string {
133:  const manager = ctx?.sessionManager;
134:  return manager?.getSessionName?.()?.trim() || manager?.getSessionId?.() || "new session";
135: }
136: 
137: function summarizeLastActivity(ctx: any): string {
138:  const branch = ctx?.sessionManager?.getBranch?.();
139:  if (!Array.isArray(branch)) return "";
140: 
141:  for (let index = branch.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
142:  const entry = branch[index];
143:  if (entry?.type !== "message") continue;
144:  const message = entry.message;
145:  const role = message?.role === "assistant" ? "agent" : message?.role === "user" ? "you" : message?.role;
146:  const content = message?.content;
147:  const text = typeof content === "string"
148:  ? content
149:  : Array.isArray(content)
150:  ? content.map((item: any) => item?.text ?? (item?.name ? `[${item.name}]` : "")).filter(Boolean).join(" ")
151:  : "";
152:  const compact = text.replace(/\s+/g, " ").trim();
153:  if (compact) return `${role ?? "message"}: ${compact}`;
154:  }
155: 
156:  return "";
157: }

159: function topicFromArgs(args: string | string[]): string {
160:  return (Array.isArray(args) ? args.join(" ") : String(args ?? "")).trim();
161: }

163: function info(ctx: any, message: string): void {
164:  ctx.ui.notify(message, "info");
165: }

167: export function createKeatingHeaderComponent(pi: any, ctx: any): (tui: any, theme: any) => any {
168:  const sections = shellCommandSections();
169:  const commandCount = sections.reduce((sum, section) => sum + section.commands.length, 0);
170: 
171:  return (_tui: any, theme: any): any => {
172:  const t = theme.fg.bind(theme);
173:  const b = theme.bold.bind(theme);
174:  const border = (text: string) => t("borderMuted", text);
175:  const dim = (text: string) => t("dim", text);
176:  const accent = (text: string) => t("accent", text);
177:  const heading = (text: string) => b(t("mdHeading", text));
178:  const text = (value: string) => t("text", value);
179: 
180:  return {
181:  render(width: number): string[] {
182:  const maxWidth = Math.max(width - 2, 1);
183:  const cardWidth = Math.min(maxWidth, 120);
184:  const innerWidth = Math.max(cardWidth - 2, 1);
185:  const contentWidth = Math.max(innerWidth - 2, 1);
186:  const outerPad = " ".repeat(Math.max(0, Math.floor((width - cardWidth) / 2)));
187:  const lines: string[] = [];
188:  const push = (line: string) => lines.push(`${outerPad}${line}`);
189:  const row = (content: string) => `${border("│")} ${padVisible(content, contentWidth)} ${border("│")}`;
190:  const emptyRow = () => `${border("│")}${" ".repeat(innerWidth)}${border("│")}`;
191:  const separator = () => `${border("├")}${border("─".repeat(innerWidth))}${border("┤")}`;
192:  const useWideLayout = contentWidth >= 72;
193: 
194:  push("");
195:  if (cardWidth >= 72) {
196:  const logoWidth = Math.max(...KEATING_ASCII_LOGO.map(line => line.length));
197:  const logoPad = " ".repeat(Math.max(0, Math.floor((cardWidth - logoWidth) / 2)));
198:  const palette = ["accent", "accent", "mdHeading", "mdHeading", "text", "text"];
199:  for (let index = 0; index < KEATING_ASCII_LOGO.length; index += 1) {
200:  push(b(t(palette[index] ?? "text", `${logoPad}${KEATING_ASCII_LOGO[index]}`)));
201:  }
202:  push("");
203:  }
204: 
205:  const versionTag = ` v${KEATING_VERSION} `;
206:  const versionGap = Math.max(0, innerWidth - versionTag.length);
207:  const versionLeft = Math.floor(versionGap / 2);
208:  push(
209:  border(`╭${"─".repeat(versionLeft)}`) +
210:  dim(versionTag) +
211:  border(`${"─".repeat(versionGap - versionLeft)}╮`),
212:  );
213: 
214:  if (useWideLayout) {
215:  const leftWidth = Math.min(38, Math.floor(contentWidth * 0.35));
216:  const dividerWidth = 3;
217:  const rightWidth = contentWidth - leftWidth - dividerWidth;
218:  const leftValueWidth = Math.max(1, leftWidth - 11);
219:  const commandNameWidth = 18;
220:  const commandDescWidth = Math.max(12, rightWidth - commandNameWidth - 2);
221:  const leftLines: string[] = [""];
222:  const rightLines: string[] = ["", heading("Teaching Workflows")];
223:  const leftLabel = (label: string, value: string, color: "text" | "dim") => {
224:  const wrapped = wrapWords(value, leftValueWidth);
225:  leftLines.push(`${dim(label.padEnd(10))} ${color === "text" ? text(wrapped[0]!) : dim(wrapped[0]!)}`);
226:  for (const line of wrapped.slice(1)) {
227:  leftLines.push(`${" ".repeat(11)}${color === "text" ? text(line) : dim(line)}`);
228:  }
229:  };
230:  const listBlock = (label: string, value: string) => {
231:  if (!value) return;
232:  leftLines.push("");
233:  leftLines.push(accent(b(label)));
234:  for (const line of wrapWords(value, leftWidth)) {
235:  leftLines.push(dim(line));
236:  }
237:  };
238: 
239:  leftLabel("model", getCurrentModelLabel(ctx), "text");
240:  leftLabel("directory", formatHeaderPath(ctx.cwd), "text");
241:  leftLabel("session", getSessionLabel(ctx), "dim");
242:  leftLines.push("");
243:  leftLines.push(dim(`${pi.getAllTools?.().length ?? 0} tools · ${commandCount} commands`));
244:  listBlock("Purpose", KEATING_SUBTITLE_LINES[0] ?? "The Hyperteacher");
245:  listBlock("Last Activity", truncatePlain(summarizeLastActivity(ctx), leftWidth * 2));
246: 
247:  for (const section of sections) {
248:  rightLines.push("");
249:  rightLines.push(accent(b(section.title)));
250:  for (const command of section.commands) {
251:  const wrapped = wrapWords(command.description, commandDescWidth);
252:  rightLines.push(`${accent(command.usage.padEnd(commandNameWidth))}${dim(wrapped[0]!)}`);
253:  for (const line of wrapped.slice(1)) {
254:  rightLines.push(`${" ".repeat(commandNameWidth)}${dim(line)}`);
255:  }
256:  }
257:  }
258: 
259:  const maxRows = Math.max(leftLines.length, rightLines.length);
260:  for (let index = 0; index < maxRows; index += 1) {
261:  push(row(
262:  `${padVisible(leftLines[index] ?? "", leftWidth)}` +
263:  `${border(" │ ")}` +
264:  `${padVisible(rightLines[index] ?? "", rightWidth)}`,
265:  ));
266:  }
267:  } else {
268:  push(emptyRow());
269:  push(row(heading(centerPlain(KEATING_SUBTITLE_LINES[0] ?? "Keating", contentWidth))));
270:  push(row(dim(centerPlain(KEATING_SUBTITLE_LINES[1] ?? "The Hyperteacher", contentWidth))));
271:  push(row(dim(centerPlain(`${pi.getAllTools?.().length ?? 0} tools · ${commandCount} commands`, contentWidth))));
272:  push(emptyRow());
273:  push(separator());
274:  for (const section of sections) {
275:  push(row(accent(b(section.title))));
276:  for (const command of section.commands) {
277:  const descWidth = Math.max(1, contentWidth - 18);
278:  push(row(`${accent(command.usage.padEnd(17))}${dim(truncatePlain(command.description, descWidth))}`));
279:  }
280:  }
281:  }
282: 
283:  push(border(`╰${"─".repeat(innerWidth)}╯`));
284:  push("");
285:  return lines;
286:  },
287:  invalidate(): void {},
288:  dispose(): void {},
289:  };
290:  };
291: }

342: function registerSpeechTool(pi: any, config: KeatingConfig): void {
343:  if (!config.speech.enabled || typeof pi.registerTool !== "function") return;
344:  if (typeof pi === "object" && pi !== null && speechToolRegistrations.has(pi)) return;
345: 
346:  pi.registerTool({
347:  name: KEATING_VOICE_TOOL_NAME,
348:  label: "Keating Voice",
349:  description: "Emit a concise voice-tagged teaching utterance for an optional conversational speech layer.",
350:  promptSnippet: "Speak brief learner-facing utterances with voice tags while the normal model continues reasoning, questioning, and verification.",
351:  promptGuidelines: [
352:  "Use keating_voice only when speech is useful for a learner-facing sentence, question, redirect, recap, or encouragement.",
353:  "Use keating_voice for short conversational delivery; keep deeper reasoning, verification, and tool-backed correction in normal text and normal tools.",
354:  "Use keating_voice tags to mark the teaching move, especially question, verify, redirect, encourage, pause, recap, and explain.",
355:  "Do not use keating_voice for citations, long derivations, file paths, code blocks, or private reasoning."
356:  ],
357:  parameters: voiceToolParameters(),
358:  async execute(_toolCallId: string, params: any) {
359:  const utterance = normalizeVoiceUtterance(params, config.speech);
360:  return {
361:  content: [{ type: "text", text: voiceTagLine(utterance) }],
362:  details: {
363:  provider: "tags-only",
364:  fastModel: config.speech.fastModel,
365:  steeringModel: config.speech.steeringModel,
366:  utterance
367:  }
368:  };
369:  }
370:  });
371: 
372:  if (typeof pi === "object" && pi !== null) {
373:  speechToolRegistrations.add(pi);
374:  }
375: }

596:  {

598:  force: { type: "boolean", description: "Set true only when the learner explicitly asks to run auto_improve again in this session" }
599:  },
600:  async (params) => {
601:  const topic = (params.topic as string) || undefined;
602:  const result = await autoImproveArtifact(getCwd(), topic, { force: params.force === true });
603:  const verdict = result.delta > 0 ? "IMPROVED" : result.delta < -0.5 ? "REGRESSED" : "NO SIGNIFICANT CHANGE";
604:  return {

606:  details: result
607:  };
608:  }

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # Keating Self-Modifying Agent Architecture Plan
2: 
3: ## Overview & Origin
4: 
5: This document captures the architectural thinking around making Keating's browser agent capable of maximum self-modification — editing its own prompts, policy, operational protocol, core logic, and tool definitions at runtime — with safe checkpoint/rollback semantics. The plan emerged from three converging ideas:
6: 
7: 1. **agentOS / Rivet** provides hosted infrastructure (actors, workflows, cron, host tools) that could close the gap between CLI-quality and browser-quality self-evolution.
8: 2. **NodePod** (open-source browser-native Node.js runtime) makes it possible to run the full agent loop — including code eval and self-editing — entirely client-side, for free, in the browser.
9: 3. **microsandbox** (Apache 2.0 microVM sandbox) provides server-side hardware-isolated execution with snapshot/restore, making the VM-in-VM checkpoint model work for the paid Keating Cloud tier.
10: 
11: The result is now a staged architecture. Keating v1 establishes the serving and runtime boundary first: browser-only work stays local by default, remote-only work has an explicit proxy path, and future NodePod, Daytona, microsandbox, or agentOS integrations fit behind the same sandbox capability interface.
12: 
13: ---
14: 
15: ## Author's Notes (Verbatim)
16:

31: ## Current State Analysis
32: 
33: ### v1 Serving Contract
34: 
35: Keating now has three explicit web serving modes:
36: 
37: | Mode | Command | Runtime meaning |
38: |---|---|---|
39: | Browser-only | `keating web --browser-only-agent [port]` | Free/local default. Browser-compatible agent work runs on the learner's device. Remote-only work returns a clear fallback. |
40: | Remote | `keating web --remote [port] --remote-provider=... --remote-endpoint=...` | Self-hosted local-first mode. The browser runs what it can locally and routes remote-only operations through the configured sandbox endpoint. |
41: | Cloud | `keating web --cloud [port]` | Canonical hosted mode. Remote-only operations route through the Keating backend at `https://keating.help` unless `--cloud-endpoint` overrides it. |
42: 
43: The browser consumes this contract through `/api/agent-runtime/config`. The same server exposes `/api/agent-runtime/remote/**` as the controlled proxy path for remote/cloud modes. In browser-only mode that proxy returns `403`, which is intentional: the free surface should not silently move arbitrary execution to a server.
44: 
45: The web agent receives two tools from this boundary:
46: 
47: - `agent_runtime`: reports mode, capabilities, remote endpoint metadata, and fallback policy.
48: - `remote_execute`: posts remote-only operations to `/api/agent-runtime/remote/execute` when configured, or returns a browser-only fallback explaining what cannot run locally.
49: 
50: This is not yet the full NodePod or microVM runtime. It is the load-bearing interface that lets those runtimes arrive as adapters instead of requiring a rewrite of the browser tools.
51: 
52: ### CLI vs Web Agent: The Self-Evolution Gap
53: 
54: | Capability | CLI (Node.js) | Web Agent (Browser) |
55: |---|---|---|
56: | LLM-in-loop benchmark/evaluation | ✅ `piCompleteJson` for sim & prompt eval | ❌ Heuristic-only (keyword scoring) |
57: | Cross-run evolution archive | ✅ JSON files on disk, accumulate across invocations | ❌ Ephemeral IndexedDB, no accumulation |
58: | Prompt `.md` file read/write | ✅ Filesystem, human-auditable | ❌ `setSystemPrompt` in-memory only |
59: | `edit_source` applying edits | ✅ CLI can apply directly with `--backup-dir` | ❌ Diff-only, user must manually apply |
60: | Separate `evolve`, `bench`, `prompt-evolve` | ✅ Discrete CLI commands | ❌ Single `auto_improve` call |
61: | `keating auto-improve --force` cooldown | ✅ 30-min cooldown with override | ❌ No cooldown enforcement |
62: | Real learner feedback blending | ❌ Not yet (future) | ✅ Already works (unique browser advantage) |
63: | Multiple optimizers | ✅ Hill-climb, MAP-Elites, GEPA, ACE | ❌ MAP-Elites + PROSPER only |
64: 
65: ### The 5 Self-Surfaces (What the Agent Can Modify About Itself)
66: 
67: | Self-Surface | Currently Mutable? | Rollback? | Why Not |
68: |---|---|---|---|
69: | Teaching policy (9 params) | ✅ `evolve` / `auto_improve` | ⚠️ Only if delta < −0.5 in `auto_improve` | No full version history |
70: | System prompt (persona + evolved) | ✅ `prompt_evolve` / `setSystemPrompt` | ❌ No version history | No revert mechanism |
71: | Operational protocol (the HOW) | ❌ Compile-time constant | — | `KEATING_OPERATIONAL_PROTOCOL` is hardcoded |
72: | Core logic (`core.ts`) | ❌ `edit_source` is diff-only in browser | — | No filesystem, no eval |
73: | Tool definitions | ❌ Static at agent creation | — | Tools are registered once, never touched |
74: 
75: ### Existing Snapshot/Rollback Mechanisms
76: 
77: | Mechanism | Scope | Trigger |
78: |---|---|---|
79: | Policy rollback | Teaching policy only | Score delta < −0.5 in `auto_improve` |
80: | Session snapshot | Messages, model, thinkingLevel | Every `agent_end` event |
81: | Session fork | Full session copy | User action |
82: | Persona reset | Persona string | User action |
83: | CLI `--backup-dir` | Source files before `edit_source` | CLI context only |
84: 
85: **What does NOT exist:** No full-state checkpoint (policy + prompt + weights + persona). No policy version history. No prompt evolution rollback. No undo for accepted `auto_improve`. No `edit_source` approval workflow in browser. No snapshot of simulation weights across runs.

89: ## Technology Survey
90: 
91: ### agentOS (Rivet) — Agent Infrastructure
92: 
93: - **By**: rivet-dev (Ironclad)
94: - **License**: Apache 2.0
95: - **npm**: `rivetkit`, `@rivet-dev/agent-os-core`
96: - **What it provides**: In-process OS kernel for AI agents — isolated V8 isolates + WASM POSIX utils + optional E2B/Daytona sandbox extensions. Actors with persistent state, durable workflows, cron scheduling, host tools (expose JS functions as agent CLI commands), session/transcript persistence via ACP, agent-to-agent delegation.
97: 
98: | Self-Evolution Primitive | agentOS Feature |
99: |---|---|
100: | Modify own code | Full read/write persistent filesystem + shell execution |
101: | Learn from history | Automatic transcript persistence + session replay (ACP) |
102: | Structured memory | SQLite for long-term knowledge storage |
103: | Act autonomously on schedule | Cron jobs for recurring self-auditing/maintenance |
104: | Improve through peer review | Agent-to-agent delegation and multi-session VMs |
105: | Extend own capabilities dynamically | Host tools (discover and call new backend functions as CLI commands) |
106: | Persist improvements across restarts | Actor storage survives sleep/wake, up to 10 GB |
107: | Modify external systems | S3, Google Drive, host directory, and sandbox mounts |
108: | Complete complex self-modification reliably | Durable workflows with retries and branching |
109: 
110: **Fit for Keating**: Best suited as the orchestration layer for the paid cloud tier — durable workflows for `auto_improve` loops, cron for scheduled evolution, host tools for LLM evaluation. Not needed for the browser tier.
111: 
112: ### NodePod — Browser-Native Node.js Runtime
113: 
114: - **By**: ScelarOrg (Scelar AI app builder)
115: - **License**: MIT + Commons Clause (source-available; can use in any product, just can't resell NodePod itself)
116: - **npm**: `nodepod` (or `@scelar/nodepod`)
117: - **Architecture**: Pure TypeScript polyfills running atop the browser's JS engine — no WebAssembly compilation of Node.js. ~600KB gzipped, ~100ms boot.
118: 
119: | Component | Implementation |
120: |---|---|
121: | Filesystem | `MemoryVolume` — in-memory POSIX-like tree with 30+ sync `fs` methods. Binary snapshots via `ArrayBuffer` for worker spawn and persistence. |
122: | Execution | `ScriptEngine` — reads files from VFS, strips TypeScript with regex, converts ESM→CJS via acorn-based AST parser, wraps in sandboxed function, runs with `eval()`. |
123: | Polyfills | 40+ Node.js built-in modules: `fs`, `http`, `child_process`, `stream`, `crypto`, `path`, `net`, `zlib`, etc. |
124: | Process model | Each process in its own Web Worker with own MemoryVolume and ScriptEngine. Up to 50 processes, 10 levels deep. VFS sync via binary snapshots. |
125: | Shell | ~3,500-line TypeScript shell with 35+ built-in commands, pipes, redirections, globs. |
126: | Package manager | Full npm client: semver resolution, parallel downloads, ESM→CJS via esbuild-wasm. Express installs in ~3-5s. |
127: | Networking | Service Worker intercepts `/__virtual__/{port}/...`, routes to owning Web Worker. WebSocket via BroadcastChannel. |
128: 
129: **Critical capabilities for Keating**:
130: - `pod.snapshot()` → binary `ArrayBuffer` capturing full VFS state — the checkpoint primitive
131: - `pod.restore(snapshot)` → ~10ms rollback to any prior state
132: - `pod.fs.writeFile()` / `pod.fs.readFile()` — agent reads and writes its own code
133: - `pod.spawn('node', [...])` with output capture — execute mutated code
134: - Snapshots can be persisted to IndexedDB for cross-session survival
135: 
136: **License caveat**: MIT + Commons Clause means you can use NodePod inside Keating Cloud, you just can't sell "NodePod hosting" as a standalone product. Since Keating's value is the hyperteacher, not the browser runtime, this is fine.
137: 
138: **Limitation**: Polyfilled `http` can't reach external APIs without a CORS proxy, so LLM calls for evaluation can't happen purely in-browser without the host app proxying them.
139: 
140: ### microsandbox — Server-Side MicroVM Sandbox
141: 
142: - **By**: Super Rad Company (YC-backed)
143: - **License**: Apache 2.0 (fully open source)
144: - **Language**: ~85% Rust
145: - **Architecture**: Real microVMs with own Linux kernel via libkrun (KVM on Linux, Apple Virtualization Framework on macOS). Not containers — hardware-level isolation.
146: 
147: | Feature | Detail |
148: |---|---|
149: | Boot | Sub-100ms cold start |
150: | Sandboxing | Hardware virtualization — own kernel, own memory, own network stack |
151: | Secret handling | Placeholder substitution — real API keys never enter the VM |
152: | Network policy | Programmable: allowlist domains, DNS rebinding protection, TOCTOU pinning, SNI verification, domain-fronting prevention |
153: | TLS interception | Auto-generated CA for host-side inspection and secret injection |
154: | Snapshots | Stopped-sandbox disk-level snapshots; new VMs boot from them instantly |
155: | Rootfs | Native passthrough (fastest) or Overlayfs with OCI image layering + COW |
156: | SDK | Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go |
157: | Agent integration | First-class MCP server + Agent Skills package (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot) |
158: | Rootless | No root or daemon required — runs as child process of calling application |
159: 
160: **Critical capabilities for Keating**:
161: - `sandbox.snapshot()` — disk-level COW layer capture, survives reboot
162: - Boot new VM from snapshot in ~100ms — the rollback primitive
163: - Full networking — LLM calls for benchmark simulation and prompt evaluation work natively
164: - Placeholder secrets — user API keys can't leak even if agent goes rogue
165: - Any Linux language — not limited to TypeScript
166: - OCI image support — pre-build `keating-agent` image with pi-agent, keating CLI, all deps

241: 
242: ## The Core Architecture: Transactional Self-Modification
243: 
244: ### The Transaction Loop
245: 
246: Every mutation — whether it's a policy tweak, a prompt rewrite, or a core-logic edit — goes through the same loop:
247: 
248: ```
249: CHECKPOINT  →  MUTATE  →  RELOAD  →  VALIDATE  →  COMMIT | ROLLBACK
250: ```
251: 
252: Concretely, as the agent runs inside the sandbox:
253: 
254: 1. **CHECKPOINT** — Before any self-modification, snapshot the VFS:
255:    - NodePod: `const cp = pod.snapshot()` — captures the full in-memory filesystem as a binary `ArrayBuffer`
256:    - microsandbox: `await sandbox.snapshot()` — captures the disk-level writable layer
257: 
258: 2. **MUTATE** — The agent writes to its own files:
259:    - `pod.fs.writeFile("/agent/prompts/learn.md", improvedPrompt)`
260:    - `pod.fs.writeFile("/agent/core/evolution.ts", patchedSource)`
261:    - `pod.fs.writeFile("/agent/protocol.md", newProtocol)`
262: 
263: 3. **RELOAD** — Hot-reload the mutated module without restarting the agent:
264:    - NodePod: the runtime re-evaluates the changed file via `eval()` (ScriptEngine already works this way — it reads from VFS on each `require()`)
265:    - microsandbox: signal the running process to reimport the module (Node.js `--watch` or a custom HMR hook)
266: 
267: 4. **VALIDATE** — Run `bench` against the post-mutation state. Compare to the pre-mutation baseline.
268: 
269: 5. **DECIDE**:
270:    - Score ≥ baseline → **COMMIT**: the mutation stands, the checkpoint is archived (not deleted — keeps history)
271:    - Score < baseline by > threshold → **ROLLBACK**: restore from checkpoint, mutation is discarded
272:    - Score within noise margin → **STASH**: mutation is saved as a candidate but not activated
273: 
274: This is structurally identical to how the CLI's `auto_improve` already works for policy, but generalized to all surfaces and backed by VM-level snapshots instead of ad-hoc re-save.
275: 
276: ### The VM-in-VM Model
277: 
278: ```
279: BROWSER:
280: ┌──────────────────────────────────┐
281: │  Host page (React app) — VM 0    │  ← renders UI, manages chat sessions
282: │  ┌────────────────────────────┐   │
283: │  │  NodePod — VM 1           │   │  ← agent runtime, all /agent/ files
284: │  │  ┌──────────────────────┐ │   │
285: │  │  │  Agent process       │ │   │  ← eval'd core.ts, tools, prompts
286: │  │  │  can write to /agent/│ │   │
287: │  │  └──────────────────────┘ │   │
288: │  │  snapshot() → ArrayBuffer │   │  ← atomic, captures all VFS state
289: │  │  restore()  → from IDB    │   │  ← rollback in ~10ms
290: │  └────────────────────────────┘   │
291: └──────────────────────────────────┘
292: 
293: SERVER:
294: ┌──────────────────────────────────┐
295: │  Host machine — VM 0             │  ← Nitro server, user auth, billing
296: │  ┌────────────────────────────┐   │
297: │  │  microsandbox — VM 1       │   │  ← real Linux microVM
298: │  │  ┌──────────────────────┐ │   │
299: │  │  │  keating agent proc  │ │   │  ← real pi-agent, real LLM calls
300: │  │  │  can write to /      │ │   │     full filesystem, any language
301: │  │  └──────────────────────┘ │   │
302: │  │  snapshot() → COW layer   │   │  ← disk-level, survives reboot
303: │  │  restore()  → boot from  │   │  ← rollback in ~100ms
304: │  └────────────────────────────┘   │
305: └──────────────────────────────────┘
306: ```
307: 
308: The key insight: **VM 1 is the unit of checkpoint/rollback, not individual files.** This means the agent can make multiple related changes (edit core logic + update protocol + tweak policy) in a single transaction, and if the combined mutation regresses, the *entire* VFS rolls back atomically. There's no partial inconsistency.

348: 
349: ## The Mutation Levels
350: 
351: ### Level 1 — Data Mutation (Prompts, Policy, Weights)
352: 
353: This is what already works today. The sandbox adds real checkpoint/rollback instead of the current partial solution. No code eval needed — the runtime reads the changed file on next access.
354: 
355: **What changes from today:**
356: - Agent writes to `/agent/prompts/learn.md` instead of calling `setSystemPrompt()`
357: - Agent writes to `/agent/policy.json` instead of calling `storage.savePolicy()`
358: - Both are preceded by `checkpoint()`, followed by `benchmark()`, and rolled back atomically on regression
359: - Version history is maintained in `registry.json`
360: 
361: ### Level 2 — Protocol Mutation
362: 
363: The agent can rewrite `/agent/protocol.md`, which replaces the currently hardcoded `KEATING_OPERATIONAL_PROTOCOL`. The reload is a re-composition: `composeKeatingSystemPrompt(persona, newProtocol)`.
364: 
365: **Safety properties:**
366: - The protocol is just text injected into the system prompt — a bad protocol degrades behavior but doesn't crash the runtime
367: - Validation = benchmark score after recomposition
368: - Rollback = restore from VFS snapshot (the old protocol.md is in the checkpoint)
369: 
370: **What changes from today:**
371: - `KEATING_OPERATIONAL_PROTOCOL` becomes a file the agent can write to instead of a compile-time constant
372: - The agent can adjust its own behavior rules: "I should check timelines less aggressively", "I should trigger auto_improve after 2 sessions not 3", "I should add a new teaching pattern for X domain"
373: 
374: ### Level 3 — Code Mutation (Core Logic + Tools)
375: 
376: The hardest and most powerful level. The agent can edit its own `core/` TypeScript, which gets re-eval'd inside the sandbox.
377: 
378: **Two safeguards:**
379: 
380: 1. **Schema guard**: Before eval'ing a mutated module, validate it produces the expected type signature. A quick `typeof result` check after eval catches broken modules before they're activated.
381: 2. **Isolation**: Mutated core modules run in a test harness first (benchmark with a fixed seed), then get promoted to the active runtime only if the harness passes.
382: 
383: ```typescript
384: // Inside the sandbox: the mutation gate
385: async function tryMutate(modulePath: string, newSource: string): Promise<boolean> {
386:   // 1. Checkpoint
387:   const cp = await checkpoint();
388: 
389:   // 2. Write
390:   fs.writeFile(modulePath, newSource);
391: 
392:   // 3. Eval in test harness (not the live runtime)
393:   const candidate = evalModule(modulePath);
394:   if (!validateSignature(candidate)) {
395:     await rollback(cp);
396:     return false;
397:   }
398: 
399:   // 4. Benchmark candidate vs baseline
400:   const candidateScore = await benchmarkWith(candidate);
401:   if (candidateScore < baselineScore - NOISE_FLOOR) {
402:     await rollback(cp);
403:     return false;
404:   }
405: 
406:   // 5. Promote to live
407:   await promote(candidate);
408:   return true;
409: }
410: ```
411: 
412: **Level 3b — Tool Mutation.** The agent can write new tools to `/agent/tools/custom-*.ts`. These get registered into the live agent session if they pass schema validation (have a `name`, `description`, `parameters` JSON schema, and `execute` function). This is how the agent "grows new capabilities" — it writes a tool, tests it, and if it works, it becomes part of its own toolset.
413: 
414: **What changes from today:**
415: - `edit_source` goes from "produce a diff the user must manually apply" to "write the file, eval it, benchmark it, commit or rollback"
416: - The agent can actually modify how `buildLessonPlan()` works, not just its input parameters
417: - New tools can emerge organically — the agent writes a `custom-quiz.ts` that generates domain-specific quiz formats, validates it, and self-registers it
418:

420: 
421: ## The Registry — Version Tracking
422: 
423: Every mutation updates the registry, which tracks which version of each module is active:
424: 
425: ```jsonc
426: {
427:   "version": 17,
428:   "baselineScore": 74.2,
429:   "lastCheckpoint": "cp-1748654321",
430:   "modules": {
431:     "prompts/learn.md": { "hash": "a1b2c3", "version": 5, "score": 76.1 },
432:     "policy.json": { "hash": "d4e5f6", "version": 8, "score": 74.2 },
433:     "protocol.md": { "hash": "g7h8i9", "version": 2, "score": 73.8 },
434:     "core/lesson-plan.ts": { "hash": "j0k1l2", "version": 1, "score": 74.2 },
435:     "core/evolution.ts": { "hash": "m3n4o5", "version": 3, "score": 75.0 },
436:     "tools/custom-quiz.ts": { "hash": "p6q7r8", "version": 1, "score": 74.5 }
437:   },
438:   "checkpoints": [
439:     { "id": "cp-1748654321", "timestamp": "2026-05-31T12:00:00Z", "score": 74.2, "label": "baseline" },
440:     { "id": "cp-1748654500", "timestamp": "2026-05-31T12:05:00Z", "score": 76.1, "label": "prompt-evolve-v5" }
441:   ]
442: }
443: ```
444: 
445: This enables:
446: - **Selective rollback**: revert just one module while keeping others
447: - **Audit trail**: see exactly what changed, when, and with what effect on the score
448: - **User visibility**: the user can browse the registry and cherry-pick changes
449: - **Agent self-awareness**: the agent can read the registry to understand its own mutation history
450: 
451: ---
452: 
453: ## Bootstrapping the Agent into the Sandbox
454: 
455: The agent doesn't start as an empty shell. At boot, the host populates the VFS with the current known-good state:
456: 
457: ```typescript
458: // Browser boot
459: const pod = await Nodepod.boot({
460:   files: {
461:     "/agent/prompts/learn.md": await loadEvolvedPrompt(storage),
462:     "/agent/policy.json": JSON.stringify(await loadActivePolicy(storage)),
463:     "/agent/weights.json": JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_WEIGHTS),
464:     "/agent/protocol.md": await loadProtocol(storage),  // was hardcoded, now a file
465:     "/agent/core/lesson-plan.ts": lessonPlanModuleSource,
466:     "/agent/core/benchmark.ts": benchmarkModuleSource,
467:     "/agent/core/evolution.ts": evolutionModuleSource,
468:     "/agent/core/prompt-eval.ts": promptEvalModuleSource,
469:     "/agent/tools/teaching.ts": teachingToolsSource,
470:     "/agent/tools/self-evolve.ts": selfEvolveToolsSource,
471:     "/agent/registry.json": JSON.stringify(initialRegistry),
472:     "/agent/agent.ts": agentRuntimeSource,  // the main loop
473:   },
474: });
475: 
476: // Take baseline checkpoint immediately
477: const baselineSnapshot = pod.snapshot();
478: await saveToIndexedDB("baseline", baselineSnapshot);
479: ```
480: 
481: Each session starts by restoring the latest checkpoint (if one exists) or from the fresh boot state.
482: 
483: ---
484: 
485: ## Browser vs Server Implementation Differences
486: 
487: | Aspect | NodePod (browser, free) | microsandbox (server, paid) |
488: |---|---|---|
489: | **Snapshot mechanism** | `pod.snapshot()` → binary ArrayBuffer in JS memory | `sandbox.snapshot()` → disk-level COW layer |
490: | **Restore** | `pod.restore(snapshot)` in ~10ms | Boot new VM from snapshot in ~100ms |
491: | **Persistence** | Save snapshots to IndexedDB (survives tab close) | Snapshots are disk artifacts (survive reboot) |
492: | **LLM evaluation** | No — polyfilled `http` can't reach external APIs without a CORS proxy | Yes — full networking, can call `api.openai.com` etc |
493: | **Code mutation scope** | TypeScript only (what NodePod's ScriptEngine can eval) | Any Linux language (Python, Go, Rust, etc) |
494: | **Secrets** | Browser-only, same-origin | microsandbox placeholder model — real keys never enter VM |
495: | **Validation quality** | Heuristic benchmarks (current `core.ts` evaluation) | Heuristic + real LLM judge (CLI-quality evaluation) |
496: | **Multi-agent** | Single process in one Web Worker | Can spawn multiple VMs for code review pipelines |
497: | **Cost** | Free (runs on user's device) | Paid (server compute) |
498: | **Cold start** | ~100ms | Sub-100ms with pre-built OCI images |
499: 
500: ---
501: 
502: ## AgentOS / Rivet Integration (Enterprise Tier)
503: 
504: For the enterprise tier, agentOS provides the orchestration layer on top of the microsandbox fleet:
505: 
506: ### Host Tools for LLM Evaluation
507: 
508: ```typescript
509: import { toolKit, hostTool } from "@rivet-dev/agent-os-core";
510: import { z } from "zod";
511: 
512: const keatingKit = toolKit({
513:   name: "keating",
514:   tools: {
515:     evaluate_prompt: hostTool({
516:       description: "LLM-evaluate a teaching prompt on 6 objectives",
517:       inputSchema: z.object({
518:         prompt: z.string(),
519:         objectives: z.array(z.string()),
520:       }),
521:       execute: async ({ prompt, objectives }) => {
522:         return await serverSideEval(prompt, objectives);
523:       },
524:     }),
525:     apply_edit: hostTool({
526:       description: "Apply a search/replace edit to a prompt file",
527:       inputSchema: z.object({
528:         path: z.string(),
529:         old: z.string(),
530:         new: z.string(),
531:       }),
532:       execute: async ({ path, old, new: newText }) => {
533:         return applySafely(path, old, newText);
534:       },
535:     }),
536:   },
537: });
538: ```
539: 
540: ### Durable Self-Improvement Workflow
541: 
542: ```typescript
543: workflow("keating-auto-improve", async (c) => {
544:   await c.step("benchmark", () => sandbox.exec("keating bench"));
545:   await c.step("evolve", () => sandbox.exec("keating evolve"));
546:   await c.step("prompt-evolve", () => sandbox.exec("keating prompt-evolve"));
547:   const postScore = await c.step("post-bench", () => sandbox.exec("keating bench"));
548:   if (postScore < preScore) await c.step("rollback", restoreSnapshot);
549: });
550: ```
551: 
552: ### Scheduled Autonomous Evolution
553: 
554: ```typescript
555: await scheduleCron({
556:   schedule: "0 */4 * * *",
557:   action: {
558:     type: "session",
559:     agent: "pi",
560:     prompt: "Run auto-improve on the weakest topic. Write the report to /agent/checkpoints/",
561:   },
562: });
563: ```
564: 
565: ---
566: 
567: ## What This Unlocks That Doesn't Exist Today
568: 
569: 1. **The agent rewrites its own teaching strategy** — not just policy params, but the lesson-plan algorithm itself. "What if I add a `reinforcement` phase after examples?" → write it, eval it, benchmark it, commit or rollback.
570: 
571: 2. **The protocol evolves** — the currently-fixed operational protocol becomes a living document. The agent can decide "I should check timelines less aggressively" and amend the protocol.
572: 
573: 3. **New tools emerge** — the agent writes a `custom-quiz.ts` tool that generates domain-specific quiz formats, validates it, and self-registers it. Next session, it's part of the toolset.
574: 
575: 4. **Cross-session accumulation** — checkpoints persist in IndexedDB (browser) or on disk (server). The agent resumes from its last known-good state, not from the compile-time default.
576: 
577: 5. **Full rollback at any point** — not just "policy reverted if delta < −0.5" but atomic VFS-level rollback for any mutation at any level.
578: 
579: 6. **The user can inspect and cherry-pick** — because every mutation is a file write with a score delta, the user can browse `registry.json`, see what the agent changed, and selectively revert individual modules.
580: 
581: ---
582: 
583: ## Implementation Roadmap
584: 
585: ### Phase 1: NodePod Integration (Browser Tier, Free)
586: 
587: **Goal**: Make the browser agent capable of maximal self-modification with checkpoint/rollback, running entirely client-side.
588: 
589: 1. Add `nodepod` as a web dependency
590: 2. Create the VFS boot sequence — populate `/agent/` from current known-good state
591: 3. Implement `checkpoint()` and `rollback()` primitives using `pod.snapshot()` / `pod.restore()`
592: 4. Persist snapshots to IndexedDB for cross-session survival
593: 5. Replace `setSystemPrompt()` with VFS writes to `/agent/prompts/learn.md`
594: 6. Replace `storage.savePolicy()` with VFS writes to `/agent/policy.json`
595: 7. Move `KEATING_OPERATIONAL_PROTOCOL` from hardcoded constant to `/agent/protocol.md`
596: 8. Implement the mutation gate (checkpoint → mutate → reload → benchmark → commit|rollback)
597: 9. Wire `edit_source` to actually apply edits inside the VFS with automatic checkpoint/rollback
598: 10. Implement tool self-registration from `/agent/tools/custom-*.ts`
599: 11. Add `registry.json` version tracking and the user-facing audit UI
600: 
601: ### Phase 2: microsandbox Integration (Server Tier, Paid)
602: 
603: **Goal**: Add LLM-in-loop evaluation, cross-session archives, and secret-safe execution.
604: 
605: 1. Pre-build `keating-agent` OCI image with pi-agent + keating CLI
606: 2. Implement per-user sandbox creation/teardown on the Nitro server
607: 3. Wire `sandbox.snapshot()` / restore for server-side checkpoint/rollback
608: 4. Add `VirtioFS` share for persistent `.keating/` directory across VM boots
609: 5. Configure network policy: allowlist `api.openai.com`, `api.anthropic.com` etc
610: 6. Inject placeholder secrets for user API keys
611: 7. Wire LLM evaluation into the mutation gate — replace heuristic scoring with `piCompleteJson` calls
612: 8. Implement cross-session archive accumulation (evolution JSON files that grow over time)
613: 9. Add multi-agent workflow: one sandbox evolves, another reviews
614: 
615: ### Phase 3: agentOS/Rivet Orchestration (Enterprise Tier, Self-Hostable)
616: 
617: **Goal**: Durable workflows, cron scheduling, multi-tenant isolation.
618: 
619: 1. Wrap the microsandbox fleet in Rivet actors
620: 2. Implement durable `auto-improve` workflow with crash recovery
621: 3. Add cron-scheduled self-improvement
622: 4. Multi-tenant isolation per customer
623: 5. Self-hostable deployment package (Docker Compose or Kubernetes)
624: 6. SLA monitoring and alerting
625: 
626: ---
627: 
628: ## Open Questions
629: 
630: - **NodePod `http` polyfill limits**: Can we make LLM calls work in-browser via a Service Worker proxy on the same origin? This would make the browser tier nearly equivalent to the server tier for evaluation quality.
631: - **Module reload granularity**: When the agent edits `/agent/core/evolution.ts`, how do we reload just that module without restarting the entire agent process? NodePod's `require()` cache needs a cache-bust mechanism.
632: - **Checkpoint storage budget**: Snapshots are binary ArrayBuffers. With active mutation, how many checkpoints can we keep in IndexedDB before hitting storage limits? Need a GC policy (keep last N, keep all with score improvement, etc).
633: - **TypeScript eval safety**: NodePod strips TypeScript with regex, then evals JS. What's the blast radius of a malformed or malicious eval? Can we limit it to pure functions that can't access the host page's DOM?
634: - **Cross-tier compatibility**: If a user evolves their agent on the free tier (heuristic eval) then upgrades to the paid tier (LLM eval), should the mutations be re-evaluated with the stronger evaluator? Likely yes, but need a migration path.
635: - **Registry conflicts**: If two sessions mutate the same module concurrently, how do we merge? Likely: last-writer-wins with checkpoint comparison, but this needs a design.
636: 
637: ---
638: 
639: ## Research Update — 2026-05-31
640: 
641: This section validates the core assumptions against current public sources and translates them into a repo-facing architecture. The major conclusion: the sandbox technologies are plausible, but Keating should first introduce a shared engine/runtime boundary. NodePod, microsandbox, and agentOS should become runtime adapters behind that boundary rather than the first architectural move.
642: 
643: ### External Runtime Findings
644: 
645: | Technology | Current finding | Keating implication |
646: |---|---|---|
647: | **NodePod** | The package is `@scelar/nodepod`. It supports browser boot with initial files, `spawn`, `install`, virtual HTTP servers, `fs.readFile` / `fs.writeFile`, and explicit `snapshot()` / `restore(snapshot)`. It requires a same-origin service worker at `/__sw__.js` for previews and virtual servers. License is MIT plus Commons Clause, with an exception for use as a component of a larger application. Sources: [NodePod GitHub README](https://github.com/ScelarOrg/NodePod), [NodePod license](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ScelarOrg/NodePod/main/LICENSE), [service worker setup](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ScelarOrg/NodePod/main/docs/sw-setup.md). | Good fit for the browser free tier's transactional VFS. It should not be treated as a security isolation boundary for hostile code; treat it as a rollbackable, same-origin worker runtime. |
648: | **microsandbox** | Public materials now explicitly advertise local, embedded microVMs, OCI images, programmable network policy, secret placeholder substitution, extensible filesystems, and snapshot/fork/restore. GitHub README marks it beta, Apache-2.0, with Linux KVM or macOS Apple Silicon requirements. Sources: [microsandbox homepage](https://microsandbox.dev/), [microsandbox GitHub](https://github.com/superradcompany/microsandbox), [microsandbox docs](https://docs.microsandbox.dev/). | Strong fit for the paid/self-hosted cloud runtime where code mutation, full toolchains, real network calls, and secret handling matter. Keep it out of the browser baseline. |
649: | **Rivet agentOS** | Current docs label agentOS preview. It provides isolated agent VMs, host tools, persistent state, file/process APIs, networking/previews, cron, workflows, multiplayer sessions, agent-to-agent pipelines, and hybrid sandbox mounting. Sources: [agentOS docs](https://rivet.dev/docs/agent-os/), [agentOS product page](https://rivet.dev/agent-os/), [Rivet workflows](https://www.rivet.dev/docs/actors/workflows/). | Use as an enterprise orchestration layer after Keating has a stable runtime contract. Do not make Phase 1 depend on a preview API. |
650: | **StackBlitz WebContainers** | The public API supports `boot`, filesystem access, `spawn`, and `mount` from a `FileSystemTree` or binary snapshot generated by `@webcontainer/snapshot`. Source: [WebContainer API](https://webcontainers.io/api). | Useful comparison point, but less direct for live rollback because the public docs emphasize mount-time binary snapshots rather than NodePod-style `snapshot()` / `restore()` on the running VFS. |
651: 
652: ### Corrections To The Earlier Assumptions
653: 
654: 1. **CLI evaluation is mixed, not purely LLM-backed.** `src/core/benchmark.ts` uses deterministic algebraic simulation by default and only calls `piCompleteJson` when `KEATING_LLM_BENCHMARK=1`. Prompt evolution attempts Pi evaluation/generation, then falls back to heuristics. The parity target should be "shared evaluator interface with capability flags", not a hard CLI=LLM/web=heuristic split.
655: 
656: 2. **The browser already has a unique data advantage.** Web storage records real learner feedback, goals, quiz results, and session-linked artifacts. CLI parity should ingest the same learner outcome schema instead of treating browser state as second-class.
657: 
658: 3. **NodePod integration needs Vite/Nitro routing work.** The Vite dev server already sets COOP/COEP headers, which helps. Production Nitro still needs an explicit `/__sw__.js` handler or emitted static asset served before the SPA fallback, plus the right JavaScript content type. NodePod previews also need COOP/COEP headers on HTML responses.
659: 
660: 4. **Self-editing live React code is the wrong first target.** Browser self-modification should initially mutate a sandboxed `/agent` VFS and promote serialized outputs: policy, prompt, protocol, command registry entries, or candidate source patches. Editing the host app's loaded bundle should remain out of scope.
661: 
662: 5. **The existing self-improvement machinery is useful but file-system-shaped.** `src/core/self-improve.ts` already has mutable source allowlists, snapshots, benchmark comparison, and accept/reject records. The next step is to extract its transaction model so filesystem, IndexedDB, NodePod VFS, and microsandbox snapshots can all implement it.
663: 
664: ---
665: 
666: ## Recommended Convergence Architecture
667: 
668: The shared architecture should have one deterministic Keating engine with runtime adapters, not separate CLI and browser implementations.
669: 
670: ### 1. Shared Engine
671: 
672: Move browser-safe, deterministic behavior into importable modules that contain no Node built-ins:
673: 
674: ```
675: src/core/engine/
676:   topics.ts
677:   policy.ts
678:   lesson-plan.ts
679:   map.ts
680:   benchmark.ts
681:   evolution.ts
682:   map-elites.ts
683:   prompt-evolution.ts
684:   engagement.ts
685:   quiz.ts
686:   mastery.ts
687:   protocol.ts
688:   commands.ts
689: ```
690: 
691: The current `web/src/keating/core.ts` should disappear over time. Vite can import browser-safe TypeScript from `src/core/engine/*`; Node-specific code stays in adapter modules.
692: 
693: ### 2. Runtime Adapters
694: 
695: Introduce a small runtime interface and implement it for CLI, current browser IndexedDB, NodePod, and microsandbox:
696: 
697: ```ts
698: export interface KeatingRuntime {
699:   readonly kind: "cli-fs" | "browser-idb" | "browser-nodepod" | "cloud-microsandbox";
700:   readonly capabilities: KeatingCapabilities;
701:   store: KeatingStore;
702:   evaluator: KeatingEvaluator;
703:   editor?: SourceWorkspace;
704:   checkpoint?: CheckpointManager;
705:   now(): Date;
706: }
707: 
708: export interface KeatingCapabilities {
709:   filesystem: boolean;
710:   sourceEditing: "none" | "proposal" | "vfs" | "host";
711:   checkpoint: "none" | "records" | "vfs-snapshot" | "vm-snapshot";
712:   llmEvaluation: "none" | "proxy" | "host";
713:   shell: boolean;
714: }
715: ```
716: 
717: The command handlers should branch on capabilities, not on "web vs CLI" directly.
718: 
719: ### 3. One Command Registry
720: 
721: Keating currently has at least four command/tool surfaces:
722: 
723: - CLI switch in `src/cli/main.ts`
724: - Pi extension commands/tools in `src/pi/hyperteacher-extension.ts`
725: - MCP tools in `src/mcp/server.ts`
726: - Browser tools in `web/src/keating/browser-tools.ts`
727: 
728: These should be generated from one registry:
729: 
730: ```ts
731: export interface KeatingCommand<I, O> {
732:   name: string;
733:   aliases?: string[];
734:   description: string;
735:   inputSchema: z.ZodType<I>;
736:   destructive: boolean;
737:   requiredCapabilities?: Partial<KeatingCapabilities>;
738:   run(runtime: KeatingRuntime, input: I): Promise<O>;
739:   renderText(output: O): string;
740: }
741: ```
742: 
743: Adapters then expose the same command set as CLI subcommands, Pi tools, MCP tools, and browser `AgentTool`s. This is the fastest route to maximal feature parity.
744: 
745: ### 4. Transactional Self-Mutation Core
746: 
747: Extract a shared mutation gate:
748: 
749: ```
750: baseline -> checkpoint -> mutate -> reload/resolve -> validate -> commit | rollback | stash
751: ```
752: 
753: Mutation targets should be typed:
754: 
755: ```ts
756: type MutationSurface =
757:   | "policy"
758:   | "weights"
759:   | "prompt"
760:   | "protocol"
761:   | "source"
762:   | "tool";
763: ```
764: 
765: Every mutation produces:
766: 
767: - registry entry
768: - checkpoint id
769: - diff or artifact summary
770: - baseline score
771: - candidate score
772: - decision
773: - rollback pointer
774: 
775: This makes CLI policy rollback, browser prompt evolution, NodePod VFS restore, and microsandbox VM restore one conceptual system.
776: 
777: ### 5. State Layout Parity
778: 
779: Use the `.keating/` layout as the canonical logical model, but let each adapter map it to its backing store:
780: 
781: | Logical path | CLI adapter | Browser IDB adapter | NodePod adapter | microsandbox adapter |
782: |---|---|---|---|---|
783: | `.keating/state/current-policy.json` | local file | `policies` store active record | `/agent/policy.json` | VM file |
784: | `.keating/prompts/learn.md` or `/agent/prompts/learn.md` | local file | `prompt-evolutions` latest record | VFS file | VM file |
785: | `.keating/state/registry.json` | local file | `registry` store | VFS file + IDB snapshot index | VM file |
786: | `.keating/state/checkpoints/*` | source snapshots | record snapshots | binary VFS snapshots in IDB | microVM snapshots |
787: | `.keating/outputs/*` | local files | artifact object stores | VFS files + exported records | VM files |
788: 
789: ---
790: 
791: ## Implementation Sequence
792: 
793: ### Phase 0 — Parity Audit And Guardrails
794: 
795: - Add a generated command parity matrix in docs or tests.
796: - Add tests that assert CLI, Pi, MCP, and web registries expose the same core command names unless a command has an explicit capability exclusion.
797: - Add a `KeatingCapabilities` type and annotate current commands.
798: 
799: ### Phase 1 — Shared Command Registry, No Sandbox Yet
800: 
801: - Create `src/core/runtime.ts` and `src/core/command-registry.ts`.
802: - Move pure command logic out of CLI/web wrappers.
803: - Adapt CLI, Pi extension, MCP server, and browser tools to the same command registry.
804: - Keep existing storage implementations for now.
805: 
806: ### Phase 2 — Browser-Safe Engine Extraction
807: 
808: - Split Node-free code from `src/core/*` into browser-safe modules.
809: - Replace slices of `web/src/keating/core.ts` with imports from the shared engine.
810: - Keep Node-only modules (`project.ts`, `paths.ts`, `pi-agent.ts`, file writers, shell commands) as CLI/server adapters.
811: - Add parity tests for `buildLessonPlan`, `runBenchmarkSuite`, policy clamping, map generation, quiz generation, engagement timeline, and prompt heuristic scoring across root and web test suites.
812: 
813: ### Phase 3 — Data/Protocol Transactions
814: 
815: - Move `KEATING_OPERATIONAL_PROTOCOL` into a shared protocol artifact with versioning.
816: - Add `registry.json` for policy, prompt, protocol, and weights.
817: - Implement checkpoint/rollback for data-level mutations in both CLI filesystem and browser IndexedDB.
818: - Update `auto_improve`, `evolve`, and `prompt_evolve` to write through the mutation gate.
819: 
820: ### Phase 4 — NodePod Browser Runtime
821: 
822: - Add `@scelar/nodepod` to the web package.
823: - Add the Vite NodePod plugin and Nitro/static handling for `/__sw__.js`.
824: - Boot `/agent` from the shared engine bundle plus current policy, prompts, protocol, weights, and registry.
825: - Implement NodePod checkpoint/rollback with `snapshot()` / `restore()`.
826: - Wire browser `edit_source` to mutate `/agent` VFS first, validate, then produce a promotable patch. Promotion to the host app remains explicit.
827: - Register custom tools only after schema validation and a benchmark smoke run.
828: 
829: ### Phase 5 — microsandbox Cloud Runtime
830: 
831: - Package Keating as an OCI image or Sandboxfile.
832: - Mount persistent `.keating/` state.
833: - Use microsandbox snapshots for full source/tool/prompt/policy transactions.
834: - Enable host-side LLM evaluation and secret placeholder injection.
835: - Re-evaluate browser-evolved mutations with stronger evaluators when users upgrade.
836: 
837: ### Phase 6 — agentOS/Rivet Orchestration
838: 
839: - Wrap the cloud runtime in durable workflows for scheduled `auto_improve`.
840: - Add reviewer/evolver multi-agent workflows.
841: - Use cron for periodic audit/evolution jobs.
842: - Keep this behind the same runtime contract so Keating can still run without Rivet.
843: 
844: ---
845: 
846: ## First Practical PR
847: 
848: The first implementation slice should avoid NodePod and microsandbox. It should make future self-modification easier without introducing a new runtime dependency:
849: 
850: 1. Add `src/core/runtime.ts` with `KeatingRuntime`, `KeatingStore`, `KeatingEvaluator`, `SourceWorkspace`, `CheckpointManager`, and `KeatingCapabilities`.
851: 2. Add `src/core/command-registry.ts` and move `plan`, `map`, `bench`, `evolve`, `prompt_evolve`, `policy`, `timeline`, `due`, `quiz`, and `outputs` into shared command descriptors.
852: 3. Update CLI and one secondary surface, preferably MCP, to consume that registry.
853: 4. Add a parity test proving the registry command names and schemas are stable.
854: 5. Only after that, adapt `web/src/keating/browser-tools.ts` to the registry.
855: 
856: That PR creates the load-bearing boundary. Once it exists, NodePod is a runtime adapter instead of a rewrite.

(End of file - total 856 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# Keating Self-Modifying Agent Architecture Plan

## Overview & Origin

This document captures the architectural thinking around making Keating's browser agent capable of maximum self-modification — editing its own prompts, policy, operational protocol, core logic, and tool definitions at runtime — with safe checkpoint/rollback semantics. The plan emerged from three converging ideas:

1. **agentOS / Rivet** provides hosted infrastructure (actors, workflows, cron, host tools) that could close the gap between CLI-quality and browser-quality self-evolution.
2. **NodePod** (open-source browser-native Node.js runtime) makes it possible to run the full agent loop — including code eval and self-editing — entirely client-side, for free, in the browser.
3. **microsandbox** (Apache 2.0 microVM sandbox) provides server-side hardware-isolated execution with snapshot/restore, making the VM-in-VM checkpoint model work for the paid Keating Cloud tier.

The result is now a staged architecture. Keating v1 establishes the serving and runtime boundary first: browser-only work stays local by default, remote-only work has an explicit proxy path, and future NodePod, Daytona, microsandbox, or agentOS integrations fit behind the same sandbox capability interface.

---

## Author's Notes (Verbatim)

### Message 1 — The Initial Question

> how would i use agentos the rivet package to get the browser agent to be more like the cli in terms of its hyperteach self editing self evolving nature?

I also need to read the README.md and AGENTS.md for claims.

1155: 		// auto_improve - Full autonomous self-improvement loop
1156: 		createTool(
1157: 			"auto_improve",
1158: 			"Run the full autonomous self-improvement loop: benchmark current policy → evolve policy via MAP-Elites → evolve prompt template → record improvement. Use this instead of calling bench/evolve/improve separately. Triggers automatically on first session and periodically thereafter.",
1159: 			{
1160: 				topic: { type: "string", description: "Optional topic to focus the improvement on" },
1161: 				force: { type: "boolean", description: "Set true only when the learner explicitly asks to run auto_improve again in this session" }
1162: 			},
1163: 			async (params) => {
1164: 				const topic = params.topic as string | undefined;
1165: 				const force = params.force === true;
1166: 				const previousPolicy = await storage.getActivePolicy();
1167: 				const alreadyRanThisSession = (await storage.getImprovementAttempts()).some(
1168: 					(attempt) => attempt.sessionId === storage.currentSessionId
1169: 				);
1170: 				if (alreadyRanThisSession && !force) {
1171: 					return "auto_improve already ran in this session. Pass force=true only if the learner explicitly asks to run it again.";
1172: 				}
1173: 
1174: 				// Snapshot NodePod VFS before any changes (if active)
1175: 				let nodePodSnapId: string | null = null;
1176: 				if (isNodePodActive()) {
1177: 					try {
1178: 						const snap = await nodePodCreateSnapshot(`auto-improve-${Date.now()}`);
1179: 						nodePodSnapId = snap.id;
1180: 					} catch {
1181: 						// ignore snapshot failures
1182: 					}
1183: 				}
1184: 
1185: 				const basePolicy = parsePolicyFromStorage(await storage.getActivePolicy());
1186: 				const learnerState = await storage.getLearnerState();
1187: 				const realOutcomes = extractBrowserOutcomes(learnerState.feedbackHistory, learnerState.topicsExplored);
1188: 				if (!hasEnoughRealData(realOutcomes)) {
1189: 					return `Not ready to auto-improve: need at least ${MIN_REAL_OUTCOMES} learner feedback signals; found ${realOutcomes.length}.`;
1190: 				}
1191: 
1192: 				// Step 1: Baseline benchmark
1193: 				const baseline = runBenchmarkSuite(basePolicy, topic, 20260401, 3, DEFAULT_WEIGHTS, realOutcomes);
1194: 				const baselineReport = benchmarkToMarkdown(baseline);
1195: 				await storage.saveBenchmark(baseline.overallScore, baselineReport, topic);
1196: 
1197: 				// Step 2: Evolve policy via MAP-Elites
1198: 				const meRun = mapElitesEvolve(basePolicy, topic, 24, 20260401, undefined, undefined, realOutcomes);
1199: 				const run = mapElitesToEvolutionRun(meRun);
1200: 				const evolveReport = mapElitesToMarkdown(meRun);
1201: 
1202: 				await storage.savePolicy(policyToMarkdown(run.bestPolicy, run.best.overallScore), true);
1203: 				const saved = await storage.saveEvolution(
1204: 					run.best.overallScore,
1205: 					JSON.stringify(run.bestPolicy),
1206: 					evolveReport,
1207: 					topic,
1208: 					JSON.stringify(run.exploredCandidates, null, 2)
1209: 				);
1210: 
1211: 				// Step 3: Evolve prompt template
1212: 				const promptBase = await getActiveKeatingPrompt(storage, "learn");
1213: 				const promptRun = evolvePromptTemplate(promptBase, "learn", 4);
1214: 				const promptReport = promptEvolutionToMarkdown(promptRun);
1215: 				const promptSaved = await storage.savePromptEvolution("learn", {
1216: 					bestScore: promptRun.best.score,
1217: 					bestPrompt: promptRun.best.prompt,
1218: 					report: promptReport,
1219: 				});
1220: 				options.setSystemPrompt?.(promptRun.best.prompt);
1221: 
1222: 				// Step 4: Post-evolution benchmark
1223: 				const evolvedPolicy = run.bestPolicy;
1224: 				const after = runBenchmarkSuite(evolvedPolicy, topic, 20260401, 3, DEFAULT_WEIGHTS, realOutcomes);
1225: 				const afterReport = benchmarkToMarkdown(after);
1226: 				const benchmarkSaved = await storage.saveBenchmark(after.overallScore, afterReport, topic);
1227: 
1228: 				// Step 5: Record improvement
1229: 				const delta = after.overallScore - baseline.overallScore;
1230: 				if (delta < -0.5) {
1231: 					await storage.savePolicy(previousPolicy?.content ?? policyToMarkdown(basePolicy, baseline.overallScore), true);
1232: 				}
1233: 				const proposalId = `auto-${Date.now().toString(36)}`;
1234: 				const improvementSaved = await storage.saveImprovementAttempt({
1235: 					proposalId,
1236: 					baselineScore: baseline.overallScore,
1237: 					afterScore: after.overallScore,
1238: 					scoreDelta: delta,
1239: 					accepted: delta > -0.5,
1240: 					targets: diagnoseBenchmark(baseline).map((s) => s.area).join(","),
1241: 					hypothesis: `Auto-improve: evolved policy (${run.acceptedCandidates.length} accepted) + evolved prompt (${promptRun.acceptedCandidates.length} accepted)`,
1242: 				});
1243: 
1244: 				const verdict = delta > 0
1245: 					? `IMPROVED by +${delta.toFixed(2)}`
1246: 					: delta < -0.5
1247: 						? `REGRESSED by ${delta.toFixed(2)} (evolved policy reverted)`
1248: 						: `NO SIGNIFICANT CHANGE (Δ${delta.toFixed(2)})`;
1249: 
1250: 				const nodePodNote = nodePodSnapId
1251: 					? `\n**NodePod snapshot:** ${nodePodSnapId} (created before improvement, can restore if needed via \`source_restore\`)`
1252: 					: "";
1253: 
1254: 				return `[artifact://evolution/${saved.id}] [artifact://prompt-evolution/${promptSaved.id}] [artifact://benchmark/${benchmarkSaved.id}] [artifact://improvement/${improvementSaved.id}]\n\nSelf-improvement complete.
1255: 
1256: **Benchmark:** ${baseline.overallScore.toFixed(2)} → ${after.overallScore.toFixed(2)} (${verdict})
1257: 
1258: **Policy Evolution (MAP-Elites):**
1259: - Accepted: ${run.acceptedCandidates.length}/${run.exploredCandidates.length} candidates
1260: - Filled cells: ${meRun.filledCellCount}/${meRun.totalCells}
1261: - Best policy: analogyDensity=${evolvedPolicy.analogyDensity.toFixed(3)} socraticRatio=${evolvedPolicy.socraticRatio.toFixed(3)} formalism=${evolvedPolicy.formalism.toFixed(3)}
1262: 
1263: **Prompt Evolution (PROSPER):**
1264: - Baseline: ${promptRun.baselineScore.toFixed(2)} → Best: ${promptRun.best.score.toFixed(2)}
1265: - Accepted: ${promptRun.acceptedCandidates.length}/${promptRun.exploredCandidates.length} candidates${nodePodNote}
1266: 
1267: **Weaknesses diagnosed:** ${diagnoseBenchmark(baseline).map((s) => s.area).join(", ") || "none"}`;
1268: 			}
1269: 		),
