1455: 		// ask_user_question - Ask the learner one or more questions as an interactive form
1456: 		createTool(
1457: 			"ask_user_question",
1458: 			"Ask the learner one or more questions as an interactive form (choices, multi-select, free text, or fill-in-the-blank). The learner fills it in and their answers are sent back automatically. Use to check understanding, gather goals/preferences, or branch the lesson. Pass `questions` for a multi-field form, or the single-question fields for a quick one-off.",
1459: 			{
1460: 				question: { type: "string", description: "A single question to ask (use `questions` for a multi-field form). For fill-in-the-blank, use ___ or {{blank}} as placeholders." },
1461: 				choices: { type: "array", items: { type: "string" }, description: "Optional answer choices for the single question" },
1462: 				multi_select: { type: "boolean", description: "Allow selecting multiple choices for the single question (default false)" },
1463: 				allow_text: { type: "boolean", description: "Allow free-text input (default: true if no choices, false if choices provided)" },
1464: 				type: { type: "string", enum: ["choice", "text", "blanks"], description: "Question type. 'choice' = multiple choice, 'text' = free text, 'blanks' = fill-in-the-blank (uses ___ or {{blank}} in question text)" },
1465: 				blanks: { type: "array", items: { type: "object", properties: { placeholder: { type: "string" }, hint: { type: "string" } } }, description: "Define blanks for fill-in-the-blank questions. Each entry corresponds to one ___ placeholder in the question text." },
1466: 				hint: { type: "string", description: "Optional hint shown below the single question" },
1467: 				intro: { type: "string", description: "Optional intro text shown above the form" },
1468: 				questions: {
1469: 					type: "array",
1470: 					description: "Multiple questions to ask at once, each with its own header and choices",
1471: 					items: {
1472: 						type: "object",
1473: 						properties: {
1474: 							header: { type: "string", description: "Short label/chip shown above the question (e.g. 'Goal', 'Approach')" },
1475: 							question: { type: "string", description: "The question text. For blanks type, use ___ or {{blank}} as placeholders" },
1476: 							choices: { type: "array", items: { type: "string" }, description: "Optional answer choices" },
1477: 							multi_select: { type: "boolean", description: "Allow selecting multiple choices (default false)" },
1478: 							allow_text: { type: "boolean", description: "Allow free-text input (default: true if no choices)" },
1479: 							type: { type: "string", enum: ["choice", "text", "blanks"], description: "Question type: choice, text, or blanks (fill-in-the-blank)" },
1480: 							blanks: { type: "array", items: { type: "object", properties: { placeholder: { type: "string" }, hint: { type: "string" } } }, description: "Blank definitions for fill-in-the-blank, one per ___ placeholder" },
1481: 							hint: { type: "string", description: "Optional hint shown below this question" },
1482: 						},
1483: 					},
1484: 				},
1485: 			},
1486: 			async (params) => {
1487: 				const toStringArray = (value: unknown): string[] | undefined =>
1488: 					Array.isArray(value)
1489: 						? value.filter((c): c is string => typeof c === "string")
1490: 						: undefined;
1491: 
1492: 				type FormField = {
1493: 					header?: string;
1494: 					question: string;
1495: 					choices?: string[];
1496: 					multi_select: boolean;
1497: 					allow_text: boolean;
1498: 					hint?: string;
1499: 				};
1500: 
1501: 				const buildField = (raw: Record<string, unknown>): FormField | null => {
1502: 					const question = String(raw.question ?? "");
1503: 					if (!question) return null;
1504: 					const choices = toStringArray(raw.choices);
1505: 					const hasChoices = !!choices && choices.length > 0;
1506: 					return {
1507: 						header: raw.header ? String(raw.header) : undefined,
1508: 						question,
1509: 						choices: hasChoices ? choices : undefined,
1510: 						multi_select: raw.multi_select === true,
1511: 						allow_text:
1512: 							typeof raw.allow_text === "boolean" ? raw.allow_text : !hasChoices,
1513: 						hint: raw.hint ? String(raw.hint) : undefined,
1514: 					};
1515: 				};
1516: 
1517: 				const fields: FormField[] = [];
1518: 				if (Array.isArray(params.questions)) {
1519: 					for (const item of params.questions) {
1520: 						if (item && typeof item === "object") {
1521: 							const field = buildField(item as Record<string, unknown>);
1522: 							if (field) fields.push(field);
1523: 						}
1524: 					}
1525: 				}
1526: 				if (fields.length === 0) {
1527: 					const single = buildField(params);
1528: 					if (single) fields.push(single);
1529: 				}
1530: 				if (fields.length === 0) return "Error: at least one question is required.";
1531: 
1532: 				const intro = params.intro ? String(params.intro) : undefined;
1533: 				const payload = JSON.stringify({ intro, questions: fields });
1534: 				const summary = fields.map((f) => f.question).join(" | ");
1535: 				return `[question] Asking learner: ${summary}\n\n<keating-question json=${JSON.stringify(payload)} />`;
1536: 			}
1537: 		),

1790: 				const lines = [
1791: 					`# Validation Result: ${result.passed ? "PASSED" : "FAILED"}`,

1808: 			"source_snapshot",

1810: 			{

1816: 				}

1831: 		// source_restore - Rollback NodePod VFS to a previous snapshot
1832: 		createTool(

1835: 			{

1837: 			},
1838: 			async (params) => {
1839: 				if (!isNodePodActive()) {
1840: 					return "NodePod sandbox is not active.";
1841: 				}
1842: 				const data = params.data;
1843: 				if (!data) {

1845: 				}
1846: 				try {

1851: 				}
1852: 			}
1853: 		),
1854: 	];
1855: 
1856: 	if (options.speech?.settings.enabled) {
1857: 		tools.push(createSpeechTool(options.speech.settings, options.speech.getApiKey));
1858: 	}
1859: 
1860: 	return tools;
1861: }

185:         value: tb.meanConfusion,

187:         region: `Domain-specific guidance for "${tb.topic.domain}" topics`,
188:         explanation: `Topic "${tb.topic.title}" (${tb.topic.domain}) causes excessive overload (confusion ${tb.meanConfusion.toFixed(2)}). The domain-specific lesson customization may need adjustment.`
189:       });
190:     }
191:     if (tb.dominantWeakness === "diagramFit") {
192:       weaknesses.push({
193:         area: `visual:${tb.topic.slug}`,
194:         metric: "diagramFit",
195:         value: tb.meanScore,
196:         file: MUTABLE_SOURCES["animation"],
197:         region: `Scene generator for ${tb.topic.domain} domain`,
198:         explanation: `Topic "${tb.topic.title}" has weak diagram fit. The animation scene for this domain may need richer visual representation.`
199:       });
200:     }
201:   }
202: 
203:   return weaknesses;
204: }
205: 
206: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
207: // Proposal generation
208: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
209: 
210: let proposalCounter = 0;
211: 
212: function generateProposalId(): string {
213:   proposalCounter += 1;
214:   const ts = Date.now().toString(36);
215:   return `improve-${ts}-${proposalCounter}`;
216: }
217: 
218: export async function generateImprovementProposal(
219:   cwd: string
220: ): Promise<ImprovementProposal> {
221:   const policy = await loadPolicy(currentPolicyPath(cwd));
222:   const benchmark = await runBenchmarkSuite(cwd, policy);
223:   const weaknesses = diagnoseFromBenchmark(benchmark);
224: 
225:   // Prioritize: pick the top 3 weaknesses by severity
226:   const sorted = weaknesses.sort((a, b) => a.value - b.value);
227:   const targets: ImprovementTarget[] = sorted.slice(0, 3).map(w => ({
228:     file: w.file,
229:     region: w.region,
230:     weakness: w.area,
231:     metric: w.metric,
232:     currentValue: w.value,
233:     rationale: w.explanation
234:   }));
235: 
236:   const hypothesis = targets.length > 0
237:     ? `Improving ${targets.map(t => t.weakness).join(", ")} should raise the overall benchmark score from ${benchmark.overallScore.toFixed(2)} by addressing the identified weak areas.`
238:     : `The benchmark score is ${benchmark.overallScore.toFixed(2)} with no severe weaknesses detected. Consider exploring novel teaching strategies.`;
239: 
240:   const instructions = await buildImprovementInstructions(cwd, targets, benchmark);
241: 
242:   return {
243:     id: generateProposalId(),
244:     timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
245:     targets,
246:     hypothesis,
247:     instructions,
248:     baselineScore: benchmark.overallScore,
249:     status: "pending"
250:   };
251: }
252: 
253: async function buildImprovementInstructions(
254:   _cwd: string,
255:   targets: ImprovementTarget[],
256:   benchmark: BenchmarkResult
257: ): Promise<string> {
258:   const lines: string[] = [
259:     "# Self-Improvement Instructions",
260:     "",
261:     "You are Keating's meta-evolution agent. Your task is to modify Keating's own source code",
262:     "to improve teaching effectiveness as measured by the benchmark suite.",
263:     "",
264:     "## Current Baseline",
265:     "",
266:     `- Overall score: ${benchmark.overallScore.toFixed(2)}`,
267:     `- Weakest topic: ${benchmark.weakestTopic}`,
268:     "",
269:     "## Knowledge Context",
270:     "",
271:     "Where applicable, leverage these Ax optimization traces to inform your changes:",
272:     `- **GEPA Pareto Front**: Check \`.keating/state/gepa-optimized.json\` for multi-objective hyperparameter tuning traces.`,
273:     `- **ACE Playbook**: Check \`.keating/state/ace-playbook.json\` for the latest prompt-learning strategy updates.`,
274:     "",
275:     "## Safety Rules",
276:     "",
277:     "1. ONLY modify files listed in the targets below. Do not touch types, config, paths, or this self-improvement module.",
278:     "2. After making changes, run `bun test ./test/*.test.ts` to verify no tests break.",
279:     "3. Run `bun src/cli/main.ts bench` to measure the impact.",
280:     "4. If the benchmark score decreases or tests fail, ROLLBACK all changes using the snapshots.",
281:     "5. Keep changes small and focused. One logical change per target.",
282:     "6. Do not change function signatures that are imported by other modules.",
283:     "7. Add a comment `// [self-improve] <proposal-id>` near each changed region.",
284:     "",
285:     "## Targets",
286:     ""
287:   ];
288: 
289:   for (let i = 0; i < targets.length; i++) {
290:     const t = targets[i];
291:     lines.push(`### Target ${i + 1}: ${t.weakness}`);
292:     lines.push("");
293:     lines.push(`- **File**: ${t.file}`);
294:     lines.push(`- **Region**: ${t.region}`);
295:     lines.push(`- **Metric**: ${t.metric} = ${t.currentValue.toFixed(2)}`);
296:     lines.push(`- **Rationale**: ${t.rationale}`);
297:     lines.push("");
298:     lines.push("**Suggested approach**: Read the file, understand the region, and make a targeted change");
299:     lines.push("that addresses the diagnosed weakness. Think about what the benchmark simulation actually");
300:     lines.push("measures and how your code change will flow through to improve the metric.");
301:     lines.push("");
302:   }
303: 
304:   lines.push("## Evaluation Protocol");
305:   lines.push("");
306:   lines.push("After applying changes:");
307:   lines.push("1. Run `bun x tsc -p tsconfig.json` — must compile clean");
308:   lines.push("2. Run `bun test ./test/*.test.ts` — all tests must pass");
309:   lines.push("3. Run `bun src/cli/main.ts bench` — record the new overall score");
310:   lines.push(`4. If new score > ${benchmark.overallScore.toFixed(2)}, the change is accepted`);
311:   lines.push(`5. If new score <= ${benchmark.overallScore.toFixed(2)}, rollback using the snapshots`);
312:   lines.push("6. Record the result using `/improve accept` or `/improve reject`");
313:   lines.push("");
314: 
315:   return lines.join("\n");
316: }
317: 
318: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
319: // Snapshot: save current state of mutable files before changes
320: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
321: 
322: export async function snapshotMutableSources(cwd: string, proposalId: string): Promise<CodeSnapshot[]> {
323:   const snapDir = join(snapshotsDir(cwd), proposalId);
324:   await mkdir(snapDir, { recursive: true });
325: 
326:   const snapshots: CodeSnapshot[] = [];
327:   for (const [_label, relativePath] of Object.entries(MUTABLE_SOURCES)) {
328:     const fullPath = join(cwd, relativePath);
329:     try {
330:       const content = await readFile(fullPath, "utf8");
331:       const snapshot: CodeSnapshot = {
332:         file: fullPath,
333:         relativePath,
334:         content,
335:         snapshotAt: new Date().toISOString()
336:       };
337:       snapshots.push(snapshot);
338:       await writeFile(join(snapDir, relativePath.replace(/\//g, "__")), content, "utf8");
339:     } catch {
340:       // file doesn't exist, skip
341:     }
342:   }
343:   return snapshots;
344: }
345: 
346: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
347: // Rollback: restore files from snapshot
348: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
349: 
350: export async function rollbackFromSnapshots(snapshots: CodeSnapshot[]): Promise<void> {
351:   for (const snapshot of snapshots) {
352:     await writeFile(snapshot.file, snapshot.content, "utf8");
353:   }
354: }
355: 
356: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
357: // Evaluate: compare before/after benchmark scores
358: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
359: 
360: export async function evaluateImprovement(
361:   cwd: string,
362:   baselineScore: number
363: ): Promise<{ afterScore: number; improved: boolean; delta: number }> {
364:   const policy = await loadPolicy(currentPolicyPath(cwd));
365:   const learnerState = await loadLearnerState(learnerStatePath(cwd));
366:   const result = await runBenchmarkSuite(cwd, policy, undefined, 20260401, 3, undefined, learnerState);
367:   const delta = result.overallScore - baselineScore;
368:   return {
369:     afterScore: result.overallScore,
370:     improved: delta > 0,
371:     delta
372:   };
373: }
374: 
375: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
376: // Record: persist an improvement attempt to the archive
377: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
378: 
379: export async function recordAttempt(
380:   cwd: string,
381:   attempt: ImprovementAttempt
382: ): Promise<void> {
383:   const archive = await loadImprovementArchive(cwd);
384:   archive.attempts.push(attempt);
385:   if (attempt.completedAt && attempt.accepted) {
386:     archive.totalAccepted += 1;
387:     archive.cumulativeImprovement += attempt.scoreDelta ?? 0;
388:   } else if (attempt.completedAt) {
389:     archive.totalRejected += 1;
390:   }
391:   await saveImprovementArchive(cwd, archive);
392: }
393: 
394: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
395: // Full pipeline: diagnose -> propose -> snapshot -> write proposal
396: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
397: 
398: export interface ImprovementArtifact {
399:   proposalPath: string;
400:   proposal: ImprovementProposal;
401:   snapshots: CodeSnapshot[];
402: }
403: 
404: export async function generateImprovementArtifact(cwd: string): Promise<ImprovementArtifact> {
405:   const dir = improvementsDir(cwd);
406:   await mkdir(dir, { recursive: true });
407: 
408:   const proposal = await generateImprovementProposal(cwd);
409:   const snapshots = await snapshotMutableSources(cwd, proposal.id);
410: 
411:   // Write the proposal as a markdown artifact the agent can read and execute
412:   const proposalPath = join(dir, `${proposal.id}.md`);
413:   const content = [
414:     `# Improvement Proposal: ${proposal.id}`,
415:     "",
416:     `**Timestamp**: ${proposal.timestamp}`,
417:     `**Baseline score**: ${proposal.baselineScore.toFixed(2)}`,
418:     `**Status**: ${proposal.status}`,
419:     "",
420:     `## Hypothesis`,
421:     "",
422:     proposal.hypothesis,
423:     "",
424:     `## Snapshotted Files`,
425:     "",
426:     ...snapshots.map(s => `- ${s.relativePath} (${s.content.length} bytes)`),
427:     "",
428:     proposal.instructions,
429:     "",
430:     "## Archive Context",
431:     ""
432:   ].join("\n");
433: 
434:   // Append prior attempt summaries for the agent's learning
435:   const archive = await loadImprovementArchive(cwd);
436:   const history = archive.attempts.slice(-5).map(a => {
437:     const status = a.accepted ? "ACCEPTED" : "REJECTED";
438:     const delta = a.scoreDelta != null ? ` (delta: ${a.scoreDelta.toFixed(2)})` : "";
439:     const targets = a.proposal.targets.map(t => t.weakness).join(", ");
440:     return `- ${a.proposal.id}: ${status}${delta} — targeted ${targets}`;
441:   });
442: 
443:   const fullContent = history.length > 0
444:     ? content + "Recent attempts (learn from these):\n\n" + history.join("\n") + "\n"
445:     : content + "No prior improvement attempts. This is the first run.\n";
446: 
447:   await writeFile(proposalPath, fullContent, "utf8");
448: 
449:   await recordAttempt(cwd, {
450:     proposal,
451:     snapshots,
452:     baselineScore: proposal.baselineScore,
453:     afterScore: null,
454:     scoreDelta: null,
455:     accepted: false,
456:     rollbackPerformed: false,
457:     completedAt: null
458:   });
459: 
460:   return { proposalPath, proposal, snapshots };
461: }
462: 
463: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
464: // Accept / Reject helpers
465: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
466: 
467: export async function acceptImprovement(
468:   cwd: string,
469:   proposalId: string,
470:   afterScore: number
471: ): Promise<void> {
472:   const archive = await loadImprovementArchive(cwd);
473:   const existing = archive.attempts.find(a => a.proposal.id === proposalId);
474:   if (existing) {
475:     existing.accepted = true;
476:     existing.afterScore = afterScore;
477:     existing.scoreDelta = afterScore - existing.baselineScore;
478:     existing.proposal.status = "accepted";
479:     existing.completedAt = new Date().toISOString();
480:     archive.totalAccepted += 1;
481:     archive.cumulativeImprovement += existing.scoreDelta;
482:     await saveImprovementArchive(cwd, archive);
483:     return;
484:   }
485: 
486:   // If not found in archive, create a new entry
487:   const proposal: ImprovementProposal = {
488:     id: proposalId,
489:     timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
490:     targets: [],
491:     hypothesis: "Accepted externally",
492:     instructions: "",
493:     baselineScore: afterScore,
494:     status: "accepted"
495:   };
496: 
497:   await recordAttempt(cwd, {
498:     proposal,
499:     snapshots: [],
500:     baselineScore: 0,
501:     afterScore,
502:     scoreDelta: null,
503:     accepted: true,
504:     rollbackPerformed: false,
505:     completedAt: new Date().toISOString()
506:   });
507: }
508: 
509: export async function rejectImprovement(
510:   cwd: string,
511:   proposalId: string,
512:   snapshots: CodeSnapshot[]
513: ): Promise<void> {
514:   await rollbackFromSnapshots(snapshots);
515: 
516:   const archive = await loadImprovementArchive(cwd);
517:   const existing = archive.attempts.find(a => a.proposal.id === proposalId);
518:   if (existing) {
519:     existing.accepted = false;
520:     existing.proposal.status = "rejected";
521:     existing.rollbackPerformed = true;
522:     existing.completedAt = new Date().toISOString();
523:     archive.totalRejected += 1;
524:     await saveImprovementArchive(cwd, archive);
525:     return;
526:   }
527: 
528:   await recordAttempt(cwd, {
529:     proposal: {
530:       id: proposalId,
531:       timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
532:       targets: [],
533:       hypothesis: "Rejected and rolled back",
534:       instructions: "",
535:       baselineScore: 0,
536:       status: "rolled-back"
537:     },
538:     snapshots,
539:     baselineScore: 0,
540:     afterScore: null,
541:     scoreDelta: null,
542:     accepted: false,
543:     rollbackPerformed: true,
544:     completedAt: new Date().toISOString()
545:   });
546: }
547: 
548: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
549: // Markdown report of improvement history
550: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
551: 
552: export function improvementHistoryToMarkdown(archive: ImprovementArchive): string {
553:   const lines = [
554:     "# Self-Improvement History",
555:     "",
556:     `- Total attempts: ${archive.attempts.length}`,
557:     `- Accepted: ${archive.totalAccepted}`,
558:     `- Rejected: ${archive.totalRejected}`,
559:     `- Cumulative score improvement: ${archive.cumulativeImprovement.toFixed(2)}`,
560:     ""
561:   ];
562: 
563:   if (archive.attempts.length === 0) {
564:     lines.push("No improvement attempts yet. Run `/improve` to start the self-improvement loop.");
565:     return lines.join("\n");
566:   }
567: 
568:   lines.push("## Attempts");
569:   lines.push("");
570: 
571:   for (const attempt of archive.attempts) {
572:     const status = attempt.accepted ? "ACCEPTED" : attempt.rollbackPerformed ? "ROLLED BACK" : "REJECTED";
573:     lines.push(`### ${attempt.proposal.id} — ${status}`);
574:     lines.push("");
575:     lines.push(`- Baseline: ${attempt.baselineScore.toFixed(2)}`);
576:     if (attempt.afterScore != null) {
577:       lines.push(`- After: ${attempt.afterScore.toFixed(2)}`);
578:       lines.push(`- Delta: ${(attempt.scoreDelta ?? 0) >= 0 ? "+" : ""}${(attempt.scoreDelta ?? 0).toFixed(2)}`);
579:     }
580:     lines.push(`- Hypothesis: ${attempt.proposal.hypothesis}`);
581:     if (attempt.proposal.targets.length > 0) {
582:       lines.push(`- Targets: ${attempt.proposal.targets.map(t => `${t.file}:${t.region}`).join(", ")}`);
583:     }
584:     lines.push("");
585:   }
586: 
587:   return lines.join("\n");
588: }

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # Browser Agent Runtime
2: 
3: Local-first agent sandbox interfaces for browser agents.
4: 
5: The package is intentionally capability-based. A NodePod-backed browser sandbox can do a lot locally: files, commands, package installs, previews, and checkpoint/rollback. It is still not a hard security boundary for hostile code. Operations that need real isolation, native binaries, brokered secrets, or durable remote compute should route to a remote adapter such as Daytona or microsandbox.
6: 
7: ## Shape
8: 
9: ```ts
10: import {
11:   createInMemoryBrowserSandbox,
12:   createDaytonaCompatSandbox,
13:   selectSandbox,
14:   withSandboxTransaction,
15: } from "@keating/browser-agent-runtime";
16: 
17: const local = createInMemoryBrowserSandbox();
18: const sandbox = createDaytonaCompatSandbox(local);
19: 
20: await sandbox.fs.uploadFile("console.log('hi')", "workspace/app.js");
21: const result = await sandbox.process.executeCommand("cat workspace/app.js");
22: console.log(result.artifacts.stdout);
23: ```
24: 
25: ## Relay Mode
26: 
27: A browser tab can host a NodePod sandbox and expose the runtime over any request/response transport:
28: 
29: ```ts
30: const handler = createSandboxRpcHandler(nodepodSandbox);
31: 
32: // Use handler behind a WebSocket, BroadcastChannel, postMessage, or fetch endpoint.
33: const remoteLookingSandbox = createRpcSandboxClient((request) => handler(request));
34: ```
35: 
36: That makes the browser sandbox look like a remote sandbox to an agent while still executing locally on the user's machine.
37: 
38: ## Adapter Plan
39: 
40: - `browser-memory`: deterministic fallback used for tests and offline capability checks.
41: - `browser-nodepod`: wraps a real `@scelar/nodepod` pod and exposes the same `AgentSandbox` contract.
42: - `remote-daytona`: a future adapter that forwards calls to Daytona's TypeScript SDK.
43: - `remote-microsandbox`: a future adapter for server/local-native microVM execution.

(End of file - total 43 lines)
</content>

1: import { unsupportedCapability } from "./errors";
2: import type {
3:   AgentSandbox,
4:   ExecResult,

9: 
10: export interface NodePodLike {
11:   fs?: {
12:     readFile?: (path: string, encoding?: string) => Promise<string | Uint8Array> | string | Uint8Array;
13:     writeFile?: (path: string, data: string | Uint8Array) => Promise<void> | void;
14:     readdir?: (path: string) => Promise<Array<string | SandboxFileInfo>> | Array<string | SandboxFileInfo>;
15:     mkdir?: (path: string, options?: { recursive?: boolean }) => Promise<void> | void;
16:     rm?: (path: string, options?: { recursive?: boolean }) => Promise<void> | void;
17:     unlink?: (path: string) => Promise<void> | void;
18:     stat?: (path: string) => Promise<Partial<SandboxFileInfo> & { isDirectory?: () => boolean }> | Partial<SandboxFileInfo> & { isDirectory?: () => boolean };
19:   };
20:   exec?: (command: string, options?: Record<string, unknown>) => Promise<unknown> | unknown;
21:   spawn?: (command: string, args?: string[], options?: Record<string, unknown>) => Promise<unknown> | unknown;
22:   port?: (port: number) => string;
23:   snapshot?: () => Promise<unknown> | unknown;
24:   restore?: (snapshot: unknown) => Promise<void> | void;
25:   dispose?: () => Promise<void> | void;
26: }
27: 
28: export interface NodePodSandboxOptions {
29:   id?: string;
30:   workDir?: string;
31:   capabilities?: Partial<SandboxCapabilities>;
32: }
33: 
34: export async function bootNodePodSandbox(options: NodePodSandboxOptions & { boot?: Record<string, unknown> } = {}): Promise<AgentSandbox> {
35:   const mod = await import("@scelar/nodepod");
36:   const factory = (mod as any).Nodepod ?? (mod as any).NodePod ?? (mod as any).default ?? mod;
37:   const pod = typeof factory.boot === "function"
38:     ? await factory.boot(options.boot ?? {})
39:     : await (mod as any).boot(options.boot ?? {});
40:   return createNodePodSandbox(pod as NodePodLike, options);
41: }
42: 
43: export function createNodePodSandbox(pod: NodePodLike, options: NodePodSandboxOptions = {}): AgentSandbox {
44:   const capabilities: SandboxCapabilities = {
45:     browserLocal: true,
46:     secureIsolation: false,
47:     nativeBinaries: false,
48:     filesystem: true,
49:     process: true,
50:     sessions: false,
51:     preview: typeof pod.port === "function",
52:     snapshots: typeof pod.snapshot === "function" && typeof pod.restore === "function",
53:     packageInstall: true,
54:     outboundNetwork: "limited",
55:     inboundNetwork: false,
56:     secrets: "browser-byok",
57:     persistence: "indexeddb",
58:     ...options.capabilities,
59:   };
60: 
61:   return {
62:     id: options.id ?? `browser-nodepod-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`,
63:     kind: "browser-nodepod",
64:     workDir: options.workDir ?? "/workspace",
65:     capabilities,
66:     fs: {
67:       async readFile(path: string, encoding?: "utf8") {
68:         assertFs(pod, "readFile");
69:         const value = await pod.fs!.readFile!(path, encoding);
70:         if (encoding === "utf8" && value instanceof Uint8Array) return new TextDecoder().decode(value);
71:         return value as any;
72:       },
73:       async writeFile(path: string, data: string | Uint8Array) {
74:         assertFs(pod, "writeFile");
75:         await pod.fs!.writeFile!(path, data);
76:       },
77:       async listFiles(path = ".") {
78:         assertFs(pod, "readdir");
79:         const entries = await pod.fs!.readdir!(path);
80:         return entries.map((entry) => {
81:           if (typeof entry !== "string") return entry;
82:           return {
83:             name: entry,
84:             path: path.endsWith("/") ? `${path}${entry}` : `${path}/${entry}`,
85:             isDir: false,
86:             size: 0,
87:             modTime: new Date().toISOString(),
88:           };
89:         });
90:       },
91:       async getFileDetails(path: string) {
92:         if (!pod.fs?.stat) {
93:           return {
94:             name: path.split("/").filter(Boolean).at(-1) ?? path,
95:             path,
96:             isDir: false,
97:             size: 0,
98:             modTime: new Date().toISOString(),
99:           };
100:         }
101:         const stat = await pod.fs.stat(path);
102:         return {
103:           name: stat.name ?? path.split("/").filter(Boolean).at(-1) ?? path,
104:           path,
105:           isDir: typeof stat.isDirectory === "function" ? stat.isDirectory() : stat.isDir ?? false,
106:           size: stat.size ?? 0,
107:           modTime: stat.modTime ?? new Date().toISOString(),
108:         };
109:       },
110:       async createFolder(path: string) {
111:         assertFs(pod, "mkdir");
112:         await pod.fs!.mkdir!(path, { recursive: true });
113:       },
114:       async deleteFile(path: string, recursive = false) {
115:         if (pod.fs?.rm) {
116:           await pod.fs.rm(path, { recursive });
117:           return;
118:         }
119:         assertFs(pod, "unlink");
120:         await pod.fs!.unlink!(path);
121:       },
122:     },
123:     process: {
124:       async executeCommand(command: string, options = {}): Promise<ExecResult> {
125:         if (pod.exec) return normalizeExecResult(await pod.exec(command, options as Record<string, unknown>));
126:         if (!pod.spawn) throw unsupportedCapability("browser-nodepod", ["process"]);
127:         const [bin, ...args] = splitCommand(command);
128:         return normalizeExecResult(await pod.spawn(bin!, args, options as Record<string, unknown>));
129:       },
130:     },
131:     preview: capabilities.preview && pod.port
132:       ? { getPreviewLink: async (port: number) => ({ url: pod.port!(port) }) }
133:       : undefined,
134:     snapshots: capabilities.snapshots && pod.snapshot && pod.restore
135:       ? {
136:           create: async (name = "nodepod-snapshot"): Promise<SandboxSnapshot> => ({
137:             id: name,
138:             createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
139:             data: await pod.snapshot!(),
140:           }),
141:           restore: async (snapshot: SandboxSnapshot) => {
142:             await pod.restore!(snapshot.data);
143:           },
144:         }
145:       : undefined,
146:     dispose: pod.dispose ? async () => { await pod.dispose!(); } : undefined,
147:   };
148: }
149: 
150: function assertFs(pod: NodePodLike, method: keyof NonNullable<NodePodLike["fs"]>): void {
151:   if (!pod.fs || typeof pod.fs[method] !== "function") {
152:     throw unsupportedCapability("browser-nodepod", ["filesystem"]);
153:   }
154: }
155: 
156: function normalizeExecResult(value: unknown): ExecResult {
157:   if (typeof value === "string") {
158:     return { exitCode: 0, stdout: value, stderr: "", result: value };
159:   }
160:   if (value && typeof value === "object") {
161:     const record = value as Record<string, unknown>;
162:     const stdout = String(record.stdout ?? record.result ?? record.output ?? "");
163:     const stderr = String(record.stderr ?? "");
164:     const exitCode = Number(record.exitCode ?? record.code ?? 0);
165:     return { exitCode, stdout, stderr, result: stdout || stderr };
166:   }
167:   return { exitCode: 0, stdout: "", stderr: "", result: "" };
168: }
169: 
170: function splitCommand(command: string): string[] {
171:   return command.match(/(?:[^\s"']+|"[^"]*"|'[^']*')+/g)?.map((part) => part.replace(/^["']|["']$/g, "")) ?? [];

247:   );
248: }

210: async function runEditCommand(cwd: string, args: string[]): Promise<void> {
211:   const fileArg = args[0];
212:   if (!fileArg) {
213:     throw commandUsage("edit", "keating edit src/core/lesson-plan.ts");
214:   }
215: 
216:   const backupDirValue = optionValue(args, "--backup-dir");
217:   const backupDir = backupDirValue ? join(cwd, backupDirValue) : undefined;
218: 
219:   // Read edit payload from stdin if piped, otherwise expect JSON file path
220:   const stdinPayload = process.stdin.isTTY
221:     ? null
222:     : await new Promise<string>((resolve) => {
223:         let data = "";
224:         process.stdin.on("data", (chunk) => { data += chunk; });
225:         process.stdin.on("end", () => resolve(data));
226:       });
227: 
228:   if (stdinPayload) {
229:     const edit = JSON.parse(stdinPayload) as { search: string; replace: string; reason?: string };
230:     if (!edit.search || typeof edit.replace !== "string") {
231:       throw new Error("stdin edit must be JSON with { search: string, replace: string, reason?: string }");
232:     }
233:     const result = await applySourceEdit(cwd, {
234:       file: fileArg,
235:       search: edit.search,
236:       replace: edit.replace,
237:       reason: edit.reason || "manual edit",
238:     }, backupDir);
239:     console.log(result.success ? `${color.ok}${result.message}${color.reset}` : `${color.err}${result.message}${color.reset}`);
240:     if (!result.success) process.exitCode = 1;
241:     return;
242:   }
243: 
244:   // Interactive mode: prompt for search and replace
245:   console.log(`${color.sepia}Interactive edit mode for ${fileArg}${color.reset}`);
246:   console.log("Paste the search block, then a line with --- then the replace block.");
247:   console.log("End with EOF (Ctrl+D) or a line with ===");
248:   console.log("");
249: 
250:   const rl = (await import("node:readline/promises")).createInterface({ input: process.stdin, output: process.stdout });
251:   const lines: string[] = [];
252:   for await (const line of rl) {
253:     if (line.trim() === "===") break;
254:     lines.push(line);
255:   }
256:   rl.close();
257: 
258:   const payload = lines.join("\n");
259:   const separatorIndex = payload.indexOf("\n---\n");
260:   if (separatorIndex === -1) {
261:     throw new Error("Could not find separator --- between search and replace blocks.");
262:   }
263: 
264:   const search = payload.slice(0, separatorIndex);
265:   const replace = payload.slice(separatorIndex + 5); // skip "\n---\n"
266: 
267:   const result = await applySourceEdit(cwd, {
268:     file: fileArg,
269:     search,
270:     replace,
271:     reason: "interactive edit",
272:   }, backupDir);
273: 
274:   console.log(result.success ? `${color.ok}${result.message}${color.reset}` : `${color.err}${result.message}${color.reset}`);
275:   if (!result.success) process.exitCode = 1;
276: }

47: 				JSON.stringify({
48: 					name: "keating-nodepod-workspace",
49: 					type: "module",
50: 					dependencies: {},
51: 					devDependencies: {},
52: 				},
53: 				null,
54: 				2)
55: 			);
56: 
57: 			await writeFileToVfs(
58: 				pod,
59: 				"/workspace/tsconfig.json",
60: 				JSON.stringify(
61: 					{
62: 						compilerOptions: {
63: 							module: "NodeNext",
64: 							moduleResolution: "NodeNext",
65: 							target: "ES2022",
66: 							strict: true,
67: 							outDir: "dist",
68: 							rootDir: ".",
69: 						},
70: 						include: ["src/**/*"],
71: 					},
72: 					null,
73: 					2
74: 				)
75: 			);
76: 
77: 			// Seed a small test runner file
78: 			await writeFileToVfs(
79: 				pod,
80: 				"/workspace/run-test.js",
81: 				`// Minimal test harness for NodePod experimentation
82: const results = [];
83: function test(name, fn) {
84:   try {
85:     fn();
86:     results.push({ name, ok: true });
87:   } catch (e) {
88:     results.push({ name, ok: false, error: e.message });
89:   }
90: }
91: function assertEq(a, b) {
92:   if (a !== b) throw new Error(\`Expected \${b}, got \${a}\`);
93: }
94: // Agent writes tests here and then runs this file
95: globalThis.test = test;
96: globalThis.assertEq = assertEq;
97: `
98: 			);
99: 
100: 			nodePodInstance = pod;
101: 			console.log("[nodepod] Booted successfully with", Object.keys(NODEPOD_BOOT_FILES).length, "source files, instanceId:", pod.instanceId);
102: 			return pod;
103: 		} catch (err) {
104: 			console.warn("[nodepod] Boot failed:", err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err));
105: 			return null;
106: 		}
107: 	})();
108: 
109: 	return nodePodBootPromise;
110: }
111: 
112: export async function teardownNodePod(): Promise<void> {
113: 	if (nodePodInstance) {
114: 		nodePodInstance.teardown();
115: 		nodePodInstance = null;
116: 		nodePodBootPromise = null;
117: 		baselineContent.clear();
118: 	}
119: }

121: /* ─── VFS helpers ───────────────────────────────────────── */
122: 
123: async function writeFileToVfs(pod: Nodepod, path: string, content: string): Promise<void> {
124: 	// Ensure directory exists
125: 	const dir = path.substring(0, path.lastIndexOf("/"));
126: 	if (dir && dir !== "/") {
127: 		try {
128: 			await pod.fs.mkdir(dir, { recursive: true });
129: 		} catch {
130: 			// may already exist
131: 		}
132: 	}
133: 	await pod.fs.writeFile(path, content);
134: }

136: /* ─── Introspection ─────────────────────────────────────── */
137: 
138: export interface NodePodMemoryStats {
139: 	vfs: { fileCount: number; totalBytes: number; dirCount: number; watcherCount: number };
140: 	engine: { moduleCacheSize: number; transformCacheSize: number };
141: 	heap: { usedMB: number; totalMB: number; limitMB: number } | null;
142: }
143: 
144: export interface NodePodInfo {
145: 	instanceId: string;
146: 	sabEnabled: boolean;
147: 	memoryStats: NodePodMemoryStats | null;
148: 	bootFileCount: number;
149: }
150: 
151: export async function nodePodInfo(): Promise<NodePodInfo | null> {
152: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
153: 	if (!pod) return null;
154: 	let memoryStats: NodePodMemoryStats | null = null;
155: 	try {
156: 		memoryStats = pod.memoryStats();
157: 	} catch {
158: 		// memoryStats may throw in some nodepod versions
159: 	}
160: 	return { instanceId: pod.instanceId, sabEnabled: pod.isSharedArrayBufferEnabled, memoryStats, bootFileCount: Object.keys(NODEPOD_BOOT_FILES).length };
161: }

163: /* ─── VFS ───────────────────────────────────────────────── */
164: 
165: export interface VfsEntry {
166: 	name: string;
167: 	path: string;
168: 	isDir: boolean;
169: 	size: number;
170: }
171: 
172: export async function nodePodReaddir(dirPath: string): Promise<VfsEntry[]> {
173: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
174: 	if (!pod) return [];
175: 
176: 	const names: string[] = await pod.fs.readdir(dirPath);
177: 
178: 	const entries = await Promise.all(
179: 		names.map(async (name) => {
180: 			const fullPath = dirPath.endsWith("/") ? `${dirPath}${name}` : `${dirPath}/${name}`;
181: 			try {
182: 				const stat = await pod!.fs.stat(fullPath);
183: 				return {
184: 					name,
185: 					path: fullPath,
186: 					isDir: !!stat.isDirectory,
187: 					size: typeof stat.size === "number" ? stat.size : 0,
188: 				};
189: 			} catch {
190: 				return { name, path: fullPath, isDir: false, size: 0 };
191: 			}
192: 		})
193: 	);
194: 
195: 	return entries.sort((a, b) => (a.isDir === b.isDir ? a.name.localeCompare(b.name) : a.isDir ? -1 : 1));
196: }
197: 
198: export async function nodePodReadTextFile(path: string): Promise<string> {
199: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
200: 	if (!pod) throw new Error("NodePod unavailable");
201: 	return pod.fs.readFile(path, "utf8");
202: }
203: 
204: export async function nodePodWriteTextFile(path: string, content: string): Promise<void> {
205: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
206: 	if (!pod) throw new Error("NodePod unavailable");
207: 	await writeFileToVfs(pod, path, content);
208: }
209: 
210: export async function nodePodDeletePath(path: string, recursive = false): Promise<void> {
211: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
212: 	if (!pod) throw new Error("NodePod unavailable");
213: 
214: 	let isDir = false;
215: 	try {
216: 		const stat = await pod.fs.stat(path);
217: 		isDir = !!stat.isDirectory;
218: 	} catch {
219: 		// stat may fail on some paths; fall through to file-first
220: 	}
221: 
222: 	try {
223: 		if (isDir) {
224: 			await pod.fs.rmdir(path, { recursive });
225: 		} else {
226: 			await pod.fs.unlink(path);
227: 		}
228: 	} catch (e) {
229: 		const msg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e);
230: 		if (msg.includes("ENOENT")) {
231: 			throw new Error(`Path not found: ${path}`);
232: 		} else if (isDir && msg.includes("ENOTEMPTY")) {
233: 			throw new Error(`Directory not empty: ${path}. Use recursive delete.`, { cause: e });
234: 		} else {
235: 			throw e;
236: 		}
237: 	}
238: }
239: 
240: export async function nodePodCreatePath(parent: string, name: string, isDir: boolean): Promise<void> {
241: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
242: 	if (!pod) throw new Error("NodePod unavailable");
243: 	const path = parent.endsWith("/") ? `${parent}${name}` : `${parent}/${name}`;
244: 	if (isDir) {
245: 		await pod.fs.mkdir(path, { recursive: true });
246: 	} else {
247: 		await writeFileToVfs(pod, path, "");
248: 	}
249: }
250: 
251: export async function nodePodExists(path: string): Promise<boolean> {
252: 	try {
253: 		const pod = await getNodePod();
254: 		if (!pod) return false;
255: 		await pod.fs.stat(path);
256: 		return true;
257: 	} catch {
258: 		return false;
259: 	}
260: }

262: /* ─── Source editing ────────────────────────────────────── */
263: 
264: export interface SourceEdit {
265: 	file: string;
266: 	search: string;
267: 	replace: string;
268: 	reason?: string;
269: }
270: 
271: export interface EditResult {
272: 	success: boolean;
273: 	file: string;
274: 	message: string;
275: 	diff?: {
276: 		linesRemoved: number;
277: 		linesAdded: number;
278: 		charDelta: number;
279: 	};
280: }
281: 
282: /**
283:  * Apply a single search/replace edit to a file in the NodePod VFS.
284:  * The search block must match exactly (including whitespace).
285:  */
286: export async function nodePodApplyEdit(edit: SourceEdit): Promise<EditResult> {
287: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
288: 	if (!pod) {
289: 		return { success: false, file: edit.file, message: "NodePod is not available" };
290: 	}
291: 
292: 	let original: string;
293: 	try {
294: 		original = await pod.fs.readFile(edit.file, "utf8");
295: 	} catch (error) {
296: 		return {
297: 			success: false,
298: 			file: edit.file,
299: 			message: `Cannot read file: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`,
300: 		};
301: 	}
302: 
303: 	const searchNormalized = edit.search.replace(/\r\n/g, "\n");
304: 	const contentNormalized = original.replace(/\r\n/g, "\n");
305: 
306: 	if (!contentNormalized.includes(searchNormalized)) {
307: 		return {
308: 			success: false,
309: 			file: edit.file,
310: 			message:
311: 				`Search block not found in file. Ensure the search text matches exactly, including ` +
312: 				`indentation and newlines. File: ${edit.file}`,
313: 		};
314: 	}
315: 
316: 	const occurrences = contentNormalized.split(searchNormalized).length - 1;
317: 	if (occurrences > 1) {
318: 		return {
319: 			success: false,
320: 			file: edit.file,
321: 			message:
322: 				`Search block appears ${occurrences} times in the file. ` +
323: 				`Edit rejected for safety — include more surrounding context to make the search unique.`,
324: 		};
325: 	}
326: 
327: 	const nextContent = contentNormalized.replace(searchNormalized, edit.replace);
328: 	await pod.fs.writeFile(edit.file, nextContent);
329: 
330: 	const linesRemoved = searchNormalized.split("\n").length;
331: 	const linesAdded = edit.replace.split("\n").length;
332: 
333: 	return {
334: 		success: true,
335: 		file: edit.file,
336: 		message: `Edited ${edit.file}: ${linesRemoved} lines removed, ${linesAdded} lines added. Reason: ${edit.reason || "unspecified"}`,
337: 		diff: {
338: 			linesRemoved,
339: 			linesAdded,
340: 			charDelta: edit.replace.length - searchNormalized.length,
341: 		},
342: 	};
343: }
344: 
345: /**
346:  * Apply multiple edits in sequence. Stops on first failure.
347:  */
348: export async function nodePodApplyEdits(edits: SourceEdit[]): Promise<{ results: EditResult[]; rollbackSnapshots: Map<string, string> }> {
349: 	const results: EditResult[] = [];
350: 	const rollbackSnapshots = new Map<string, string>();
351: 
352: 	for (const edit of edits) {
353: 		const pod = await getNodePod();
354: 		if (!pod) {
355: 			results.push({ success: false, file: edit.file, message: "NodePod unavailable" });
356: 			break;
357: 		}
358: 
359: 		let currentContent: string;
360: 		try {
361: 			currentContent = await pod.fs.readFile(edit.file, "utf8");
362: 		} catch {
363: 			currentContent = "";
364: 		}
365: 
366: 		if (!rollbackSnapshots.has(edit.file)) {
367: 			rollbackSnapshots.set(edit.file, currentContent);
368: 		}
369: 
370: 		const result = await nodePodApplyEdit(edit);
371: 		results.push(result);
372: 
373: 		if (!result.success) {
374: 			break;
375: 		}
376: 	}
377: 
378: 	return { results, rollbackSnapshots };
379: }
380: 
381: /**
382:  * Rollback files in NodePod VFS to their pre-edit state.
383:  */
384: export async function nodePodRollbackEdits(snapshots: Map<string, string>): Promise<void> {
385: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
386: 	if (!pod) throw new Error("NodePod unavailable");
387: 	for (const [file, content] of snapshots) {
388: 		await pod.fs.writeFile(file, content);
389: 	}
390: }
391: 
392: /**
393:  * Compute diff between the current VFS content and the baseline.
394:  */
395: export function nodePodComputeDiff(): Array<{ file: string; baseline: string; current: string; changed: boolean; charDelta: number }> {
396: 	const diffs: Array<{ file: string; baseline: string; current: string; changed: boolean; charDelta: number }> = [];
397: 	for (const [file, baseline] of baselineContent) {
398: 		// We can't read current from sync context; this is a placeholder for async diff
399: 		// The actual diff is done by source_diff tool which reads async
400: 	}
401: 	return diffs;
402: }
403: 
404: /**
405:  * Async diff for a single file.
406:  */
407: export async function nodePodDiffFile(file: string): Promise<{ baseline: string | undefined; current: string; changed: boolean; charDelta: number } | null> {
408: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
409: 	if (!pod) return null;
410: 	const baseline = baselineContent.get(file);
411: 	let current: string;
412: 	try {
413: 		current = await pod.fs.readFile(file, "utf8");
414: 	} catch {
415: 		return null;
416: 	}
417: 	return {
418: 		baseline,
419: 		current,
420: 		changed: current !== baseline,
421: 		charDelta: current.length - (baseline?.length ?? 0),
422: 	};
423: }
424: 
425: /**
426:  * Get a list of all files that differ from baseline.
427:  */
428: export async function nodePodChangedFiles(): Promise<Array<{ file: string; charDelta: number }>> {
429: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
430: 	if (!pod) return [];
431: 	const changed: Array<{ file: string; charDelta: number }> = [];
432: 	for (const [file, baseline] of baselineContent) {
433: 		try {
434: 			const current = await pod.fs.readFile(file, "utf8");
435: 			if (current !== baseline) {
436: 				changed.push({ file, charDelta: current.length - baseline.length });
437: 			}
438: 		} catch {
439: 			// file deleted or unreadable
440: 		}
441: 	}
442: 	return changed;
443: }

445: /* ─── Shell ─────────────────────────────────────────────── */
446: 
447: export interface ShellSession {
448: 	id: string;
449: 	command: string;
450: 	args: string[];
451: 	stdout: string;
452: 	stderr: string;
453: 	exitCode: number | null;
454: 	running: boolean;
455: 	startedAt: number;
456: 	durationMs: number | null;
457: 	ok: boolean;
458: 	result: string;
459: }
460: 
461: export async function nodePodRunCapturing(command: string, args: string[] = []): Promise<ShellSession> {
462: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
463: 	if (!pod) throw new Error("NodePod unavailable");
464: 
465: 	const id = crypto.randomUUID?.() ?? `sess-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 7)}`;
466: 	const startedAt = performance.now();
467: 
468: 	const session: ShellSession = {
469: 		id,
470: 		command,
471: 		args,
472: 		stdout: "",
473: 		stderr: "",
474: 		exitCode: null,
475: 		running: true,
476: 		startedAt,
477: 		durationMs: null,
478: 		ok: false,
479: 		result: "",
480: 	};
481: 
482: 	try {
483: 		const proc = await pod.spawn(command, args, { cwd: "/workspace" });
484: 		proc.on("output", (text: string) => {
485: 			session.stdout += text;
486: 		});
487: 		proc.on("error", (text: string) => {
488: 			session.stderr += text;
489: 		});
490: 		proc.on("exit", (code: number) => {
491: 			session.exitCode = code;
492: 		});
493: 		await proc.completion;
494: 	} catch (e) {
495: 		session.stderr += `\n[spawn error: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}]`;
496: 		session.exitCode = session.exitCode ?? -1;
497: 	} finally {
498: 		session.running = false;
499: 		session.durationMs = Math.round(performance.now() - startedAt);
500: 		session.ok = session.exitCode === 0;
501: 		session.result = session.stdout || session.stderr;
502: 	}
503: 	return session;
504: }
505: 
506: /**
507:  * Run a Node.js script by writing it to a temp file and executing it.
508:  */
509: export async function nodePodRunScript(code: string, filename = "/workspace/_agent_script.js"): Promise<ShellSession> {
510: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
511: 	if (!pod) throw new Error("NodePod unavailable");
512: 	await writeFileToVfs(pod, filename, code);
513: 	return nodePodRunCapturing("node", [filename]);
514: }

516: /* ─── Snapshots ─────────────────────────────────────────── */
517: 
518: // In-memory snapshot log for the visualizer to persist across probes
519: const snapshotLog: Array<{ id: string; instanceId: string; createdAt: string; data: unknown }> = [];
520: 
521: export function getSnapshotLog(): typeof snapshotLog {
522: 	return [...snapshotLog];
523: }
524: 
525: export async function nodePodCreateSnapshot(name?: string): Promise<{ id: string; instanceId: string; createdAt: string; data: unknown }> {
526: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
527: 	if (!pod) throw new Error("NodePod unavailable");
528: 	const snap = pod.snapshot();
529: 	const entry = {
530: 		id: name ?? `snapshot-${Date.now()}`,
531: 		instanceId: pod.instanceId,
532: 		createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
533: 		data: snap,
534: 	};
535: 	snapshotLog.unshift(entry);
536: 	// cap to 20
537: 	snapshotLog.splice(20);
538: 	return entry;
539: }
540: 
541: export async function nodePodRestoreSnapshot(data: unknown): Promise<void> {
542: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
543: 	if (!pod) throw new Error("NodePod unavailable");
544: 	await pod.restore(data as ReturnType<typeof pod.snapshot>);
545: }
546: 
547: /* ─── Validation Bridge ─────────────────────────────────── */
548: 
549: /** Track the most recent snapshot per file path for rollback. */
550: const preEditSnapshotByFile = new Map<string, { id: string; data: unknown }>();
551: 
552: /**
553:  * Transpile TypeScript source to JavaScript using a fast regex-based
554:  * transpiler suitable for the Keating core modules.
555:  *
556:  * This handles:
557:  *   - Type annotations on vars, params, returns
558:  *   - Interface / type declarations
559:  *   - Generic parameters
560:  *   - Import type / export type
561:  *   - ESM import extensions (.ts → .js)
562:  */
563: export function transpileTsToJs(source: string): string {
564: 	let js = source;
565: 
566: 	// Remove `import type` and `export type` lines
567: 	js = js.replace(/^\s*import\s+type\s+[^;]+;/gm, "");
568: 	js = js.replace(/^\s*export\s+type\s+[^;]+;/gm, "");
569: 
570: 	// Remove standalone type aliases
571: 	js = js.replace(/^\s*type\s+\w+[^=;]*=\s*[^;]+;?\s*$/gm, "");
572: 
573: 	// Remove interface declarations (simple single-line and multi-line)
574: 	js = js.replace(/^\s*interface\s+\w+\s*\{[^}]*\}\s*$/gm, "");
575: 	js = js.replace(/\binterface\s+\w+\s*\{[\s\S]*?\n\}\s*$/gm, "");
576: 
577: 	// Remove generic type parameters from functions, classes, methods
578: 	js = js.replace(/(function|class|method)\s+(\w+)<[^>]+>/g, "$1 $2");
579: 
580: 	// Remove variable type annotations: const x: number = 5 → const x = 5
581: 	// Be careful not to match inside strings — this is best-effort
582: 	js = js.replace(/(\b(?:const|let|var)\s+\w+)\s*:\s*[^=;]+(?=[=;])/g, "$1");
583: 
584: 	// Remove parameter type annotations in function signatures
585: 	// (conservative: only match simple identifiers, not destructuring)
586: 	js = js.replace(/(\(|,)\s*(\w+)\s*:\s*[^,)\]]+/g, "$1$2");
587: 
588: 	// Remove return type annotations: ): SomeType { → ) {
589: 	js = js.replace(/\)\s*:\s*[^{]+\{/g, ") {");
590: 
591: 	// Remove `as` type assertions
592: 	js = js.replace(/\s+as\s+\w+/g, "");
593: 
594: 	// Convert .ts import extensions to .js
595: 	js = js.replace(/from\s+["']([^"']+)\.ts["']/g, 'from "$1.js"');
596: 	js = js.replace(/require\(["']([^"']+)\.ts["']\)/g, 'require("$1.js")');
597: 
598: 	// Remove access modifiers and declare keywords
599: 	js = js.replace(/\b(declare|readonly|private|protected|public)\b/g, "");
600: 
601: 	// Clean up excess blank lines
602: 	js = js.replace(/\n{3,}/g, "\n\n");
603: 
604: 	return js;
605: }
606: 
607: /**
608:  * Given a .ts path in the VFS, transpile it to .js and write the JS
609:  * counterpart so NodePod's `require()` can load it.
610:  */
611: export async function writeJsCounterpart(tsPath: string): Promise<string> {
612: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
613: 	if (!pod) throw new Error("NodePod unavailable");
614: 
615: 	const jsPath = tsPath.replace(/\.ts$/, ".js");
616: 	const tsContent = await nodePodReadTextFile(tsPath);
617: 	const jsContent = transpileTsToJs(tsContent);
618: 	await writeFileToVfs(pod, jsPath, jsContent);
619: 	return jsPath;
620: }
621: 
622: export interface ValidationResult {
623: 	passed: boolean;
624: 	exitCode: number | null;
625: 	stdout: string;
626: 	stderr: string;
627: 	durationMs: number;
628: 	restored: boolean;
629: 	message: string;
630: }
631: 
632: /**
633:  * Validate a source edit by running a test script inside NodePod.
634:  * If the test fails and a pre-edit snapshot exists for the file,
635:  * automatically rolls back to that snapshot.
636:  */
637: export async function nodePodValidateEdit(
638: 	tsFilePath: string,
639: 	testScript: string,
640: 	options: { autoRollback?: boolean; snapshotLabel?: string } = {}
641: ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
642: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
643: 	if (!pod) throw new Error("NodePod unavailable");
644: 
645: 	const { autoRollback = true } = options;
646: 
647: 	// Step 1: snapshot current state (if not already snapshotted)
648: 	let snapId: string | null = null;
649: 	if (autoRollback && !preEditSnapshotByFile.has(tsFilePath)) {
650: 		try {
651: 			const snap = await nodePodCreateSnapshot(`validate-${Date.now()}`);
652: 			preEditSnapshotByFile.set(tsFilePath, { id: snap.id, data: snap.data });
653: 			snapId = snap.id;
654: 		} catch {
655: 			// snapshot failed, continue without rollback safety
656: 		}
657: 	}
658: 
659: 	// Step 2: transpile the TS file to JS so require() works
660: 	let jsPath: string;
661: 	try {
662: 		jsPath = await writeJsCounterpart(tsFilePath);
663: 	} catch (e) {
664: 		return {
665: 			passed: false,
666: 			exitCode: null,
667: 			stdout: "",
668: 			stderr: `Transpile failed: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}`,
669: 			durationMs: 0,
670: 			restored: false,
671: 			message: `Could not transpile ${tsFilePath} to JS.`,
672: 		};
673: 	}
674: 
675: 	// Step 3: run the test script
676: 	const started = performance.now();
677: 	const filename = `/workspace/_validate_${Date.now()}.js`;
678: 	const session = await nodePodRunScript(testScript, filename);
679: 	const durationMs = Math.round(performance.now() - started);
680: 
681: 	const passed = session.exitCode === 0;
682: 
683: 	// Step 4: rollback on failure if snapshot exists
684: 	let restored = false;
685: 	if (!passed && autoRollback) {
686: 		const saved = preEditSnapshotByFile.get(tsFilePath);
687: 		if (saved) {
688: 			try {
689: 				await nodePodRestoreSnapshot(saved.data);
690: 				restored = true;
691: 			} catch {
692: 				// restore failed, leave partial state
693: 			}
694: 		}
695: 	}
696: 
697: 	const message = passed
698: 		? `Validation passed. (${durationMs}ms)`
699: 		: restored
700: 			? `Validation failed (exit ${session.exitCode}). Automatic rollback to snapshot ${snapId ?? "unknown"} completed. Edit was reverted.`
701: 			: `Validation failed (exit ${session.exitCode}). No snapshot available for rollback — edit remains in place.`;
702: 
703: 	return {
704: 		passed,
705: 		exitCode: session.exitCode,
706: 		stdout: session.stdout,
707: 		stderr: session.stderr,
708: 		durationMs,
709: 		restored,
710: 		message,
711: 	};
712: }
713: 
714: /** Clear the pre-edit snapshot tracker (e.g. after a successful session). */
715: export function clearPreEditSnapshots(): void {
716: 	preEditSnapshotByFile.clear();
717: }
718: 
719: /* ─── Virtual servers ───────────────────────────────────── */
720: 
721: export function nodePodGetPreviewUrl(port: number): string | null {
722: 	if (!nodePodInstance) return null;
723: 	return nodePodInstance.port(port) ?? null;
724: }
725: 
726: /* ─── execute bridge (for tools + visualizer probes) ─────── */
727: 
728: export async function nodePodExecute(operation: string, payload: unknown): Promise<unknown> {
729: 	const pod = await getNodePod();
730: 	if (!pod) {
731: 		throw new Error("NodePod is not available");
732: 	}
733: 
734: 	switch (operation) {
735: 		case "runtime.ping": {
736: 			const info = await nodePodInfo();
737: 			return { ok: true, ...info, timestamp: Date.now() };
738: 		}
739: 
740: 		case "shell.exec": {
741: 			const p = (payload || {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
742: 			const command = typeof p.command === "string" ? p.command : "";
743: 			const args = Array.isArray(p.args) ? p.args.map(String) : [];
744: 			const session = await nodePodRunCapturing(command, args);
745: 			return session;
746: 		}
747: 
748: 		case "script.run": {
749: 			const p = (payload || {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
750: 			const code = String(p.code ?? "");
751: 			const filename = typeof p.filename === "string" ? p.filename : "/workspace/_agent_script.js";
752: 			if (!code.trim()) throw new Error("script.run requires payload.code");
753: 			return await nodePodRunScript(code, filename);
754: 		}
755: 
756: 		case "fs.read": {
757: 			const p = (payload || {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
758: 			const path = String(p.path ?? "");
759: 			if (p.encoding === "utf8") {
760: 				const content = await nodePodReadTextFile(path);
761: 				return { content, encoding: "utf8" };
762: 			}
763: 			// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
764: 			const content: Uint8Array = await (pod.fs.readFile(path) as any);
765: 			return { content: uint8ArrayToBase64(content), encoding: "base64" };
766: 		}
767: 
768: 		case "fs.write": {
769: 			const p = (payload || {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
770: 			const path = String(p.path ?? "");
771: 			const encoding = p.encoding === "base64" ? "base64" : "utf8";
772: 			const contentStr = String(p.content ?? "");
773: 			const data: string | Uint8Array = encoding === "base64" ? base64ToUint8Array(contentStr) : contentStr;
774: 			await pod.fs.writeFile(path, data);
775: 			return { ok: true, path };
776: 		}
777: 
778: 		case "snapshot.create": {
779: 			const p = (payload || {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
780: 			const result = await nodePodCreateSnapshot(typeof p.label === "string" ? p.label : undefined);
781: 			return result;
782: 		}
783: 
784: 		case "snapshot.restore": {
785: 			const p = (payload || {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
786: 			const data = p.data;
787: 			if (!data) throw new Error("snapshot.restore requires payload.data");
788: 			await nodePodRestoreSnapshot(data);
789: 			return { ok: true, instanceId: pod.instanceId };
790: 		}
791: 
792: 		default:
793: 			throw new Error(`Unsupported NodePod operation: ${operation}`);
794: 	}
795: }
796: 
797: function uint8ArrayToBase64(bytes: Uint8Array): string {
798: 	const binary = Array.from(bytes)
799: 		.map((b) => String.fromCharCode(b))
800: 		.join("");
801: 	return btoa(binary);
802: }
803: 
804: function base64ToUint8Array(base64: string): Uint8Array {
805: 	const binary = atob(base64);
806: 	return Uint8Array.from(binary, (c) => c.charCodeAt(0));
807: }

(End of file - total 807 lines)
</content>

parts.text:
I now have a comprehensive view of the codebase. Let me compile my findings into a detailed, honest audit. I need to cover:

1. Core self-evolution machinery (auto-improve, evolve, improve)
2. Browser agent runtime capabilities
3. Pi extension self-evolution
4. Docs claims vs reality
5. TODOs and gaps

Let me structure this clearly.

# Keating Self-Evolution Audit: Code vs. Docs vs. Reality

This audit compares what the codebase actually does against what documentation claims. Verified against source code on 2026-06-09.

---

## 1. Core Self-Evolution Machinery (`src/core/`)

### What Currently Works

**`autoImproveArtifact()` in `src/core/project.ts` (lines 350-497)**
- Runs a deterministic, fully local pipeline: `bench → evolvePolicy → evolvePrompt → bench`
- Implements a **30-minute cooldown** with `--force` override (verified in `assertAutoImproveAllowed`, lines 338-348)
- **Automatic rollback**: If `delta < -0.5`, restores both the previous policy JSON and the previous evolved prompt `.md` file (lines 396-399)
- Writes rich observability artifacts: JSON transaction log + Mermaid flow diagram + markdown report
- All file operations are real `node:fs/promises` calls to `.keating/outputs/` and `.keating/state/`

**`evolvePolicyArtifact()` in `src/core/project.ts` (lines 216-237)**
- Requires `MIN_REAL_OUTCOMES` learner feedback signals (default: enough real data threshold from `benchmark-real.ts`)
- Uses **MAP-Elites** via `mapElitesEvolve()` (not the legacy hill-climber from `evolution.ts`)
- Persists evolved policy to `current-policy.json` and syncs to `policy-archive.json`
- Returns report path + trace path + best score

**`evolvePromptArtifact()` in `src/core/project.ts` (lines 239-245)**
- Delegates to `writePromptEvolutionArtifacts()` in `prompt-evolution.ts`
- PROSPER-style selection across 6 objectives (voice, diagnosis, verification, retrieval, transfer, structure)
- Writes both `.md` report and `.evolved.md` snapshot
- Successive runs resume from prior `*.evolved.md` artifact (accumulating evolution)

**`improveArtifact()` / `self-improve.ts` (lines 398-588)**
- Generates a **code-level** improvement proposal diagnosing benchmark weaknesses
- Snapshots mutable source files before changes (`snapshotMutableSources`, line 322)
- Maintains an `improvement-archive.json` with accept/reject history
- CLI supports `improve accept <id>` and `improve reject <id>` with snapshot rollback

### What Is Stubbed/Missing

- **No actual LLM-in-loop evaluation**: `benchmark.ts` uses deterministic algebraic simulation by default. `piCompleteJson` is only called when `KEATING_LLM_BENCHMARK=1`. The prompt evolution falls back to heuristic scoring when Pi evaluation fails.
- **No cross-run archive accumulation for web**: The CLI has `policy-archive.json` and `improvement-archive.json` on disk. The browser has no equivalent persistent archive that survives across sessions for evolution history.
- **`evolvePolicy()` (legacy hill-climber) is still exported but unused**: The CLI and Pi extension both call `mapElitesEvolve`, not the legacy `evolvePolicy`. The file `evolution.ts` exports both but `project.ts` only imports `evolutionToMarkdown` and `evolvePolicy` (legacy) is dead code in the main flow.
- **No `ax-optimizer.ts` or GEPA optimizer integration visible**: `evolution.ts` imports `optimizePolicy` from `./ax-optimizer.js` and re-exports `evolveWithGEPA`, but `project.ts` never calls it. It appears to be an unused seam.

---

## 2. Browser Agent Runtime (`web/`)

### What Currently Works

**`browser-tools.ts` — self-evolution tools (lines 922-1361)**
- `bench`: Runs deterministic `runBenchmarkSuite()` against browser-stored policy
- `evolve`: Runs `mapElitesEvolve()` + saves to IndexedDB via `storage.saveEvolution()`
- `auto_improve`: Runs the full `bench → evolve → prompt_evolve → bench` loop (lines 1155-1269)
- `prompt_evolve`: PROSPER-style prompt evolution with `evolvePromptTemplate()` (lines 1334-1359)
- `improve`: Generates improvement proposals via `generateImprovementProposal()` (lines 1271-1294)
- **Per-session cooldown**: Browser `auto_improve` enforces "already ran this session" gating (lines 1167-1172), but **NOT** the 30-minute wall-clock cooldown that the CLI enforces
- **Automatic rollback on regression**: Reverts policy if delta < -0.5 (line 1230-1232)
- `setSystemPrompt` callback updates the live agent prompt when prompt evolution succeeds (line 1220, 1353)

**`nodepod-runtime.ts` — NodePod integration (lines 1-807)**
- NodePod boots lazily in background on agent creation (`bootNodePod()` in `useKeatingAgent.tsx`, line 292)
- VFS is pre-populated with ~68 bundled source files (`nodepod-boot-files.ts`)
- Source editing tools: `source_edit`, `source_diff`, `run_script`, `validate_source_edit`, `source_snapshot`, `source_restore`
- Auto-transpiles `.ts` to `.js` for NodePod `require()` (`transpileTsToJs`, line 563)
- Snapshot/restore using `pod.snapshot()` and `pod.restore()` (lines 525-545)
- `agent_runtime` tool reports NodePod presence when active (lines 568-577)

### What Is Stubbed/Missing

| Claim | Reality |
|-------|---------|
| "Full self-modification at all 3 mutation levels" (docs) | **Only Level 1 works reliably.** Policy and prompt mutations are real. Code mutation is confined to NodePod VFS — the agent can edit `/workspace/src/core/*.ts` but **those changes never automatically flow back into the live browser app.** There is no HMR or module reload from NodePod into the React host. |
| "Checkpoint/rollback for code mutations" | NodePod `snapshot()`/`restore()` works for VFS files, but the browser agent **does not automatically validate and rollback code edits** as part of `auto_improve`. The `validate_source_edit` tool exists but must be called manually by the agent. |
| "Tool self-registration" | Not implemented. Custom tools written to `/workspace/tools/` are never registered into the live Agent toolset. |
| "LLM-in-loop evaluation in browser" | **Not possible.** NodePod's polyfilled `http` cannot reach external APIs without a CORS proxy. The docs explicitly admit this limitation. Benchmarks and prompt evals use deterministic heuristics only. |
| "Cross-session archive accumulation" | **No.** Browser IndexedDB stores individual artifacts but there is no `.keating/state/policy-archive.json` equivalent that accumulates across `auto_improve` runs. Each browser session starts fresh for evolution history. |
| "Registry.json version tracking" | **Not implemented.** No `registry.json` exists in browser or CLI. |
| "Remote execute through Keating Cloud" | The `remote_execute` tool exists (lines 601-684) and posts to `executionEndpoint`, but the actual server-side sandbox provisioner is **not implemented**. `agent-runtime.ts` loads config but there is no real microsandbox/Daytona backend wired up. The docs admit: "The remote microVM provisioner is not yet implemented" (CHANGELOG line 94). |
| `packages/browser-agent-runtime/` | Exports interfaces and a **memory sandbox** (`createInMemoryBrowserSandbox`) that only supports `pwd`, `ls`, `echo`, and `cat`. The NodePod adapter (`createNodePodSandbox`) is a real wrapper around `@scelar/nodepod`. Daytona and microsandbox adapters are **future stubs** in the README only. |

---

## 3. Pi Extension (`src/pi/hyperteacher-extension.ts`)

### What Currently Works

- Registers all self-evolution tools: `auto_improve`, `evolve`, `improve`, `prompt_evolve`, `prompt_eval`
- Slash commands: `/auto-improve`, `/evolve`, `/improve`, `/prompt-evolve`
- `auto_improve` tool description claims it "Triggers automatically on first session and periodically thereafter" — but **the Pi extension does NOT actually auto-trigger it**. The handler only runs when the user or agent explicitly calls the tool/command.
- `improve` command supports `history`, `accept <id>`, `reject <id>` subcommands via CLI; Pi extension only supports `history` via the `action` parameter (no accept/reject in Pi tool handler)
- WeakSet deduplication for tool registration (lines 295-297)

### What Is Stubbed/Missing

- **No auto-trigger logic**: The claim "Triggers automatically on first session" in the tool description (line 595) is aspirational. There is no cron, no session-count gating, no automatic invocation in `session_start` (lines 1024-1055).
- **No `improve accept/reject` in Pi tools**: The `improve` Pi tool handler (lines 625-641) only branches on `action === "history"`. The CLI has full accept/reject with snapshot rollback.
- **No `edit` command in Pi**: The CLI has `keating edit` with interactive search/replace. There is no Pi equivalent.

## 4. Docs Claims vs. Reality

### `docs/self-modifying-agent-architecture.md` (the architecture plan)

| Claim in Docs | Verified Reality |
|---------------|------------------|
| "Three explicit web serving modes: Browser-only, Remote, Cloud" | **Partially true.** `src/cli/main.ts` parses `--browser-only-agent`, `--remote`, `--cloud` flags. `serveWeb` accepts these modes. But Remote/Cloud are just config flags — there is no actual remote sandbox provisioner. |
| "V1 serving contract... already names browser-only, remote, and cloud" | **True for API surface, false for backend.** The browser consumes `/api/agent-runtime/config`. The proxy path exists. But `/api/agent-runtime/remote/**` has no real handler. |
| "NodePod: full self-modification at all 3 mutation levels" | **False for automatic operation.** Level 1 (policy/prompt) works. Level 2 (protocol) is still a compile-time constant (`KEATING_OPERATIONAL_PROTOCOL`). Level 3 (code) requires manual agent use of `source_edit` + `validate_source_edit` and does not reload the host app. |
| "microsandbox: server-side microVM sandbox" | **Not implemented.** Mentioned only in docs/CHANGELOG. No code references to microsandbox execution. |
| "agentOS/Rivet: enterprise orchestration" | **Not implemented.** Only mentioned in architecture doc. |
| "Transactional self-modification: CHECKPOINT → MUTATE → RELOAD → VALIDATE → COMMIT \| ROLLBACK" | **Only for policy in `auto_improve`.** No generalized transaction gate. No registry. No automatic validation gate for code mutations. |
| "VM-in-VM model" | **Partially.** NodePod runs in a Web Worker (VM 1 inside browser VM 0), but there is no automatic checkpoint/validation/rollback loop wired to self-evolution. |
| "Implementation Roadmap Phase 1: NodePod Integration" | **Partially done.** NodePod boots, VFS is populated, source editing tools exist. Missing: auto-reload, registry, automatic mutation gate, custom tool registration. |

### `README.md`

- Claims `packages/browser-agent-runtime/` has "Daytona-shaped compatibility, NodePod adapter seam, RPC relay protocol" — **true for interfaces, false for real backends**. Only `browser-memory` and `browser-nodepod` exist.
- Claims browser agent has `agent_runtime` and `remote_execute` — **true**.
- Claims "self-hosted remote mode... with remote-only work" — **aspirational**. The flag exists; the backend does not.

### `AGENTS.md`

- Lists `keating auto-improve [topic]` with "30-min cooldown, auto-rollback on regression" — **true for CLI, false for browser** (browser has per-session cooldown only, not 30-min wall-clock).
- Does not mention that browser evolution is heuristic-only, lacking LLM-in-loop eval.

## Summary: Honest Bottom Line

**What actually works today:**

- The CLI is the only fully functional self-evolution environment. Policy evolution, prompt evolution, `auto-improve` with cooldown and rollback, and code-level improvement proposals with filesystem snapshots all work deterministically and locally.
- The **browser can run the same deterministic algorithms** (MAP-Elites, PROSPER) and stores artifacts in IndexedDB. It can evolve policy and prompts, and roll back on regression.
- **NodePod is wired up** and boots lazily. The agent can edit source files in a sandboxed VFS, run scripts, and snapshot/restore. This is real.

**What is fundamentally missing:**

1. **No automatic code mutation in the browser.** NodePod edits stay in the sandbox. The host React app never reloads mutated modules. The agent must manually use `source_edit` + `run_script` + `source_restore` — there is no automatic `auto_improve` gate for code.
2. **No cross-session persistence for evolution archives.** Browser `auto_improve` runs are isolated per session for archive history (though individual artifacts persist).
3. **No LLM-in-loop evaluation anywhere by default.** Everything is deterministic heuristic scoring unless `KEATING_LLM_BENCHMARK=1` is set in the CLI environment.
4. **Remote/cloud sandbox backends are pure configuration.** The `agent_runtime` and `remote_execute` tools exist, but there is no actual Daytona, microsandbox, or Keating Cloud provisioner behind them.
5. **The architecture roadmap (Phase 1-6) describes a system that does not exist yet.** NodePod integration is maybe 40% complete. Registry, protocol mutation, custom tools, and automatic transactional validation are all unimplemented.

**The biggest gap:** The docs and tool descriptions imply the browser agent is "self-governing" and "automatically" self-improves. In reality, the browser agent can only self-improve when explicitly told to run `auto_improve`, and code-level self-modification is a manual, multi-step process confined to a non-reloading sandbox.
